articles.title: Session 1
articles.content:
We woke up, had breakfast and then tried to find the Gigglinh Gryphon.

Once we got there we did some new-unit orientation and training by attacking target dummies

articles.title: Session 10
articles.content:
[h2]A Shady Meeting (Pt 2) - Green Spotted Boogaloo[/h2]
We arrive at @[Duhm's Rooms](landmark:f83b9e6a-745d-459a-b363-a6759cb707bd), [b]Turin's[/b] former home. It is a large Tudor framed building with multiple entrances. We go to the common area, and see a couple people down on their luck. We order drinks from the old man behind the bar. He gives us a key to room 42.

We search the hall, and go to room 42. We use the key to go into the room. It is dark inside. The room has a table and two chairs and doesn't look like it is used for much. A muscular human male asks us to shut the door. He seems young (twenty-ish) with shaggy long hair and very well dressed. He says he needs help with an affair of the heart. We turn on the light. On the chair is a common cloak.

"The one that I love will not have me unless we can be married in a sanctified church. But we can't do it in the city". He needs help looking for a church, and a small group to be taken there to be married. He first heard about us when we rescued Billy. He says her family will object, his foster father might be okay, and the @[Young Stallions](organization:7340b23d-2c0c-45fd-8222-13b00aa2760c) would be against it because she is from a merchant family. It would be appreciated if we can do this within a month. There is a small town (Kilwich) about 15 miles to the East that has a church that might still be sanctified.

We can leave a message with @[Burbas "the Finder"](landmark:d33174fa-bee4-4d56-800c-437deac58e0f)  who hangs out near the Porpidolphus Fountain in @[Thieves' Market](settlement:8b3bf96f-ea9a-4e39-8c3f-bda804df5cdb). Tell him is a message for Antonio. If he has a message for you, he will send it to @[The Giggling Gryphon](landmark:29260de7-ef14-4aa9-a123-5d0858f00927).

We return to the Griffon. Our dots are teal. There is a centaur cab parked outside. @[Porky](person:e267fbb1-0d16-45d6-88c5-2bae4738a1dc) rushes to greet us. He says that something happened in the @[City of the Dead](landmark:bbfddc91-1a44-4b0b-8161-0e768eba0ef4),  a former cemetery turned into a park and the cab is from @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) to take us.

During the ride, the centaur is going as fast as they can, going around North Caravan street, we lurch onto two wheels, and feel a familiar lurch. We come to walking up @[Studio Lane](landmark:a3a5c599-c67d-41a6-b3be-d94b4271a605), the park on our left. It seems calm. We see several watch members. Some seem pale, others queasy.

Just inside the front gates is a small hut and the watch sergeant. There were three members of @[The Jackals](organization:723f61da-04ae-4811-ab6b-93821210d4e3) gang who were murdered. We go look at the morgue. There are three bodies laid out. Probably a tiefling, human, and dwarf. They have been stabbed and slashed all over. Their faces look like they have been crushed in a vice. The consensus is that they were a group of grave robbers who stumbled onto something they shouldn't have. Martin suggests the other guards get a priest to cast gentle repose.

We go to examine the scene. [b]Arada[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] try to recreate what happened. [b]Turin[/b] looks for tracks. [b]Turin[/b] notices a glow globe is glowing a different color, much brighter than the others. It looks like this is the end of a running battle. The prints are all shod, no animals, probably the victims.

[b]Eugene[/b] ritual casts [i]Detect Magic[/i], Martin stays with him while the rest move on. There are several more blood areas ahead. The driftglobes are magic. Except for three near a large statue of a fallen warrior. Which engage in combat. One zaps [b]Eugene[/b]. Turin shoots it and hurts it badly. The battle rages, with the Will O Wisps doing damage, and the party hitting back (turns out they are hard to hit). But we eventually prevail.

We continue to follow the blood. There is blood splatter heading into the @[Geronde Family](organization:f19cd890-5d54-44cd-b4bf-d301b974af87) mausoleum. "What you fear, we master." Outside is a diorama of many classic monsters.

The family goes back to the founding of Haven. Ekhan Geronde was the founder of the family, maybe 5 generations back. They were monster slayers, including a red dragon. The current matriarch, @[Rowena Geronde](person:c44f3336-7f83-4e89-a5c8-78896cc3f650) is bringing the family back, especially through her role as an enchantress. The door was broken from the outside, probably by the grave robbers.

We enter the mausoleum…

articles.title: Session 11
articles.content:
[h2]Mirror, mirror on the ... wait, where did everybody go?[/h2]
We enter the mausoleum and have a short talk about why we are here. We all agree that we want to find what is going on, and not necessarily hunt monsters. But if they are causing problems, we'll do what we need to keep people safe.

The blood stain seems to come through two doors to our right. The door leads to a room that is surprisingly well lit. A fire is burning in the fireplace, tapestries show people fighting monsters. The trail of blood leads to the double doors on the north end. Past that the blood trail splits up.

We find a workshop, with the scent of embalming oil. Tool storage, and coffins, one of which is open. We hear a sound from behind us. We see before us what appears to be an old man. [b]Martin[/b] tries to make nice. A rumbling voice says "leave now and never return." [b]Penelope[/b] throws [b]Arada[/b] in front of the spectral figure. [b]Arada[/b] tries a charm offensive on the figure. The figure roars and tries to frighten us. [b]Penelope[/b] is frightened. We have a good old fashioned Haven Standoff. [b]Martin[/b] tries to leave, and it attacks. All hell breaks loose and we kill it. Well, it was dead. But now it is more dead. We continue to search. The tools look like they have not been used in a long time.

We keep exploring and there are two doors to the left. The blood goes on north. To the north is an open room with coffins and tapestries that are undamaged. The blood seems to end here. In the background we can hear whispering. We search the room, and [b]Turin[/b] seems to notice that the blood comes from behind the tapestry. The spectre reappears. Again we quickly dispatch it. Three zombies come out of the coffins. One goes down quickly, but the other two are meaner. They do a little damage to [b]Turin[/b], but are dispatched. We put the bodies back in the coffins, just in case. [b]Arada[/b] finds a secret door behind a tapestry which is locked.

Behind the secret door is a small octagonal room with a door to the south. There are 11 pedestals with urns on the top. On the north wall is a mahogany cabinet, and on the west wall is a large ornate mirror in a golden frame. [b]Penelope[/b] looks in the mirror and disappears. In [b]Penelope[/b]'s place we see a strange woman (Rani). She claims to be nobility. [b]Martin[/b] lies and says that it is the year 1420, and that @[The Jackals](organization:723f61da-04ae-4811-ab6b-93821210d4e3) had come out of the tomb as ghouls and defeated half of the city. She surrenders. [b]Martin[/b] deliberately looks in the mirror and vanishes. Another Jackal (Haral) pops out who recognizes Raini. [b]Turin[/b], [b]Eugene[/b], and [b]Arada[/b] tie them up.

[b]Eugene[/b] covers the mirror with a tapestry. [b]Arada[/b], [b]Eugene[/b], and [b]Turin[/b] have an argument about what to do while [b]Martin[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] are trapped. [b]Eugene[/b] goes to get help. [b]Turin[/b] hog ties the people with [b]Arada[/b]. [b]Turin[/b] examines the cabinet. There are statues, history books, etc. [b]Arada[/b] however finds a trapped secret door that is trapped. She poisons herself accidentally, but it open the secret room. She finds a story of the family. Including the fact that they were cursed. Also she finds the instructions to the mirror. [b]Arada[/b] sends [b]Turin[/b] to get [b]Eugene[/b] since they will need a mage. "…living family member be trapped, they may be summoned forth using the phrase 'ka karangatia e au te' followed by their name." [b]Eugene[/b] brings [b]Martin[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] out. Kin Semorst, Indra Glide, and Tornas Icebringer.

[b]Martin[/b] confirms [b]Arada[/b] is poisoned with a slow acting poison. [b]Martin[/b] cures her wounds, but not her poison. The party realizes we still haven't found the monster. We take the prisoners out to the other guards and return to try to find the monster. [b]Turin[/b] and [b]Arada[/b] go through the south door. The door slams shut behind them and there is a hissing noise. [b]Penelope[/b] breaks the door down. We see [b]Arada[/b] on [b]Turin[/b]'s shoulders.

We go down some stairs under the other passage and go up some stairs. We enter another room, deeper in the mausoleum…

articles.title: Session 12
articles.content:
[h2]After midnight...[/h2]
… And then we feel a sudden lurch that makes us feel totally nauseous…

We are back in the room of with the mirror. It is still covered. Our spots are light blue now. The door is closed not broken down. We've gone back in time.

[b]Arada[/b] unlocks the door and we spike it open. She goes to where the trap is. She tries to disable it. She feels and hears something break. She thinks it might be disarmed. [b]Penelope[/b] tries to test the pressure plate with [b]Martin[/b]'s mace. [b]Martin[/b] goes to test. The trap still goes off but he is unaffected.

We go into a small chamber with decorated coffins. There is another door leading on. [b]Arada[/b] leads the way searching for traps. We find a corridor that leads to a secret door in the workshop. We go to the two doors in the main corridor we didn't open before.

It is a small room, with a fancy sword on top of an altar. Behind the altar is a suit of armor. It is a chapel dedicated to @[Mandos](person:74a5fbe1-efe8-4723-b118-35eec9865113). [b]Martin[/b] looks for blood on the armor and sword and finds none. [b]Eugene[/b] casts Detect Magic to look for magic. The armor is magical, the altar is not, the sword is. Nothing else in the room is (other than the wall sconces - continual flame).

We go back outside. There is a crowd gathered. It is getting towards twilight and about 5:30 am. Sergeant Brell. We should tell the @[Geronde Family](organization:f19cd890-5d54-44cd-b4bf-d301b974af87). We decide to go to the GG to get Thom to patch up [b]Arada[/b] and then go to @[The Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b).

We find Thom in her lab and she tells us to take [b]Arada[/b] upstairs. Work mode Thom interrogates her. She looks at the wound, mutters to herself, and goes to her room to get a potion and tells [b]Arada[/b] to drink it. [b]Arada[/b] drinks it and feels godawful and throws up. She tells [b]Arada[/b] to take it easy for the rest of the day or things could not go well.

We ask Mrs Clever if she has any to go food. She moderately ascerbically gives us some apples and bread and [b]Arada[/b] a much of coffee maybe?

We go to @[The Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b) and go to the @[Geronde Family residence](landmark:aaa37936-24d9-4028-897c-f461d6e7d60c) (I21 on the map). There is an ornate entrance. We go to the front door. We meet a teenaged girl sneaking out. She introduces herself as Adrienne. We ask to speak to her parents. She tells us her grandma is home. She lets us inside and goes to get the grandmother.

Grandmother comes down and is lady @[Rowena Geronde](person:c44f3336-7f83-4e89-a5c8-78896cc3f650). We gave her the book. We gave her a summary of what happened. She is more concerned with information containment. [b]Martin[/b] tries to calm her down. She tells us to never talk about this to anyone.

We go to the hospital. Our spots are now violet. The folk there are unable to help us. They are praying for divine help, and hope we are able to take advantage when it comes.

We decide to go back to the GG and get some rest and drink together.

As we take a long rest we level up and are now third level.

When we wake up, our spots are black.

We give @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) the full debrief.
[ul]
[li]Spots
[li]Mausoleum and @[Geronde Family](organization:f19cd890-5d54-44cd-b4bf-d301b974af87)
[li]Young noble and marrying
[li]Black Hand
[/ul]

@[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) mentions she thinks [b]Arada[/b] may know what is going on and that [b]Arada[/b] stole something from Lady @[Karilinia Inturisdan](person:f4f671ed-3c46-47d1-aa37-4b13ca12d3c9) who is in the @[Circle Chamber](organization:61e67a7d-70ca-4b03-818c-e3f7beb47566).

She also wipes the spot off our face with a hanky.

She is afraid of a Thieves guild war in town. She also wants us to play along with Antonio for now.

[b]Eugene[/b] identifies the two things we had found that were magical.

The rock is a +1 stone of protection

The gloves are … to be determined

articles.title: Session 13
articles.content:
[h2]Let's play a drinking game. Rule 1: Drink.[/h2]
After the  @[City of the Dead](landmark:bbfddc91-1a44-4b0b-8161-0e768eba0ef4) adventures   we are given the past day and a half off. [b]Arada[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] goes drinking, [b]Eugene[/b] scribes some spells, and we otherwise relax.

Towards evening, [b]Penelope[/b], [b]Arada[/b], [b]Martin[/b], and Turn decide to go out drinking. We end up at @[The Seasick Parrot](landmark:4ed94cfc-6c8c-48e1-8c53-08a9e4f4b480), which turns out to be a lot nicer than we expected being on the docks next to a tattoo parlor. It is a local drinking hole with music and conversation.

Our waitress, Brandi Justcallifyouneedme, brings us some of the house ale, which is strong and bad. We toast to not being dead. [b]Arada[/b] vaguely recognizes the barkeep from the guild. [b]Penelope[/b] ask for a song from the bard who hits on the redheads.

There is a nearby table of dwarves that is drunk, that [b]Penelope[/b] really wants to have start a fight so we can finish it. Fortunately the dwarf launches itself at [b]Penelope[/b]. The desired bar fight ensues. We kick their butts and [b]Martin[/b] heals the injured (mostly them).

Meanwhile, [b]Eugene[/b] has a dinner with  @[Thom](person:c39bd715-1d56-409a-b5eb-8fc7c142ab95) to learn more about her. He learns that Thom loves to experiments with potions, and volunteers that the party would be glad to test them out. He also learns that Thom doesn't remember her childhood, and was raised by @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de), who is like her foster mom. She also explains healing is a calling for her. He also learns that @[Porky](person:e267fbb1-0d16-45d6-88c5-2bae4738a1dc) and @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) apparently go way back.

She also says that if we are ever out of the city, we could get her some ingredients.

On our way home from the bar, a kid runs out of an alley and pushes into [b]Penelope[/b], then runs away down a different alley. We decide to follow the kid but lose track of him.

We need to meet the girl and see the church.

articles.title: Session 14
articles.content:
[h2]Burning The Midnight Oil[/h2]
In the morning we get an envelope that says "[i]Tonight, she'll be there at @[The Midnight Oil](landmark:6f2a6d5f-3968-4e40-be09-b7453b820a70) with friends.[/i]"

The day's patrol is uneventful, and that evening we go out to @[The Midnight Oil](landmark:6f2a6d5f-3968-4e40-be09-b7453b820a70) which is in the north section along Nobles Way. The bar sign is a bed on fire.

We get a table in the inn. It is full. We order a round of brown ale. We also ask if the waitress has ever seen Antonio and his girlfriend around. We describe Antonio and she thinks she vaguely recognizes him. [b]Penelope[/b] thinks she sees a possible candidate near the fireplace. [b]Arada[/b] goes over to scope her out. She overhears a bunch of standard late teens social gossip. The girl is a little better off than her compatriots.

[b]Eugene[/b] considers using [i]Detect Thoughts[/i] to see if the girl is our mark. Before that, he chooses to use the message cantrip to message her. They have a short mental conversation. She's skeptical, and wants to know who we are. [b]Eugene[/b] says we are friends of @[Claudia de la Riis](person:646e36a4-cc41-4a91-8076-5af2dcb007fb). She suspects we are sent by @[Paul de la Riis](person:61db5c85-e436-459e-926e-17ad7e116b03) who is from a large merchant family and very much an enemy of the @[Young Stallions](organization:7340b23d-2c0c-45fd-8222-13b00aa2760c).

Adelade sidles next to [b]Arada[/b] and asks why she is hiding down there. [b]Arada[/b] says people watching. A drunk halfling comes to make conversation. The halfling named Prithin tries a pickup line on [b]Arada[/b]. [b]Arada[/b] uses this as an excuse to go sit with the girls.  Meanwhile the tiefling who was at the bar acts as a bouncer to kick Prithin out. 

The girl is Isabella. There is also @[Marita](person:7c9dab1a-8221-41ca-83f6-0d3b49331e37), Sybella, and Agatha. [b]Arada[/b] introduces herself as Notia. Marita says her boyfriend is great and his name is Antonio. [b]Arada[/b] asks if they are serious. [b]Arada[/b] shmoozes. Marita tells a story that matches with the story we are expected. [b]Arada[/b] offers to help if she can. She says she is a romantic and girls gotta stick together. [b]Arada[/b] offers to help without pushing too hard. She gets mild resistance, but seems to think three days from now makes sense to meet again.

[b]Arada[/b] fills us in and suggests we leave.

A drunk half elf approaches us and tries to pick a fight with [b]Martin[/b] then [b]Turin[/b]. [b]Martin[/b] sends him to the girls and leaves. We leave before the bar fight.

We go back to the Giggling Griffon. As we start to go to sleep, we feel the ground shake. We get the lurching sensation in our stomachs. This one feels really bad. [b]Martin[/b] runs to the window.


[i]Suddenly it is  five days later . . .[/i]


[h2]Meanwhile, the same different day[/h2] 
Earthday, 6th of Flocktime.

We talk to @[Porky](person:e267fbb1-0d16-45d6-88c5-2bae4738a1dc) who says we have been acting normal the past five days. We talk to @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) and explains what is going on. She suggests talking to Thom. Thom says she heard from a person at the university that this has been experienced by a few people. Professor Gearhardt is the one to talk to of the Department of Chronology. He has beeen hanging out at @[The Library](landmark:1fad35ca-bd59-446d-865d-6f5201603543).

@[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) says we were going to take the weekend to go investigate the lover's situation. We rent a cart and horse and a horse for [b]Turin[/b], and head east.

Towards the end of the day we get to the town we thing is Killwich. There is a rundown pub called the Wandering Griffon. Entering there are a few trestle tables and benches. The barkeep is unfriendly. We talk about the church and he says it is a bad place. It was a church to @[Mandos](person:74a5fbe1-efe8-4723-b118-35eec9865113) the god of Death, but it has been taken over by a group of strangers. The Innkeeper also says that his sister has gone missing, and would pay a reward if we could save her.

We head east, through fog and a rough track.

In the clearing we see a large church. On the front there are four stone statues of creatures with wings. We go around, there is a side building tucked beside the tower with a corridor connecting it to another side building. There are lights inside the church and the buildings. To the west side, there is a green glow coming from a window. We see flickering of shadows through the window, but it is hard to tell what it is.

It is about 40 feet from tower to tower, and 80 feet from front to back.

We head back to the inn. In the morning we go back.

articles.title: Session 15
articles.content:
We wake in the morning and decide to head to the church. We discuss if we need a cover story but the truth will suffice. As we wander north of the village, the closer we get to where the church would be, it gets foggier. [b]Turin[/b] detects that we are being followed. Possible two two legged things, or one four legged thing, or an eight legged thing walking on its hind legs.

It realizes it has been spotted and fades into the mist, but we can tell it is pacing us. Eventually the fog clears and we come to the church. [b]Arada[/b] and [b]Martin[/b]  go to look in the windows. Peeking in the window of the building on the east, we see a bedroom, cupboard, and person seated at a desk. Further on is a blue stained glass window we can't see through. There are more windows on the back, and side. Martin thinks there is an evil presence. Martin continues to sense evil through the windows. The gargoyle statues also ping evil. Martin also is pretty sure the church is still consecrated.

One of the gargoyles comes in looking menacing. We attack it, doing some damage, but they are tough. Hits from the group smashes it to pieces. We keep an eye on the others as Penelope knocks on the door.

The front doors are 12 foot tall ironwood doors. As we approach, we feel a shiver of cold. There is no answer, so we enter. We hear chanting from the inside of the church, and the doors close behind us. We are attacked by rats. Big rats but not unusually large rats. Turin notices that the rat's eyes are totally blank. Suddenly [b]Penelope[/b] loses the ability to speak, as if a magical electric field called Discord is borked. When she smashes the rat, she can speak again.

We go in, and see a couple nuns who are chanting. [b]Turin[/b] is overtaken by the chanting and prostrates himself on the ground. The nuns move to attack.

[b]Eugene([/b] uses his first ever second level spell to web the area. [b]Martin[/b] puts a sanctuary around [b]Turin[/b] and goes to shield. The nuns are attacking anyway. [b]Turin[/b] snaps out of it. We are trying to stop the nuns. After a bit of combat, an older woman at the front says she will take care of this. [b]Martin[/b] tries to calm emotions. One of the nuns seem confused as to what is going on. We down all the nuns, to several of our chagrins.

The old woman at the front is still mad at us. [b]Eugene[/b] tries to sleep her but she shrugs it off. [b]Penelope[/b] tries to subdue her, she dodges it. She casts a spell hurting [b]Penelope[/b] badly. Eugene ends up killing her.

To our left we see a ghost, on top of a glowing sigil. It approaches us. [b]Penelope[/b] swings, but her sword goes right through it. [b]Turin's[/b] hunter's mark does mild damage. The ghost attacks [b]Martin[/b] but misses. [b]Arada[/b] tries to distract the ghost, since it seems only magic hurts it. Fortunately [b]Eugene[/b] has magic and splats it.

On the body, [b]Martin[/b] finds a symbol of @[Mandos](person:74a5fbe1-efe8-4723-b118-35eec9865113) that has been corrupted. She also has a dagger that gives off a dark aura of necrotic energy that is obvious to all. [b]Eugene[/b] searches a desk and finds a couple books. Two appear to be from a set of books containing treatises on how to reanimate a body. [b]Turin[/b] finds a not so secret door near the altar. On it is a dagger and some candles. Possibly with some dried blood. Behind the altar is a leather book that gives off a weird vibe. [b]Penelope[/b] notices the glowing sigil is still glowing. [b]Martin[/b] gives [b]Penelope[/b] his mace and holds her beer. [b]Penelope[/b] shatters the crystal which becomes a lot of smaller glowing pieces.

[b]Eugene[/b] investigates the three books. The books seem to be possessed. They are a trilogy, "[i]The Books of New Living[/i]" and are about how to reanimate bodies.

articles.title: Session 16
articles.content:
[h2]Deeper Into The Church[/h2]
[b]Arada[/b] takes the creepy dagger.  [b]Eugene[/b] takes the starter set of the necromantic arts. He also takes a small number of crystal shards.

[b]Arada[/b] examines the secret door. It is not trapped or locked, but she hears the sound or armor on the other side. We go through the door and there is a figure in dark armor. It draws its paired long and short swords. [b]Eugene[/b] panics and launches a fire bolt, mildly singing it. It swings at [b]Penelope[/b] and hits for serious damage.
 
[b]Arada[/b] leaps over it, and gibbets it in the kidneys. The body has the two swords which [b]Penelope[/b] takes. [b]Martin[/b] examines the body of the half-elf. 

The door on the far side is not locked. [b]Arada[/b] hears the sounds of moaning.She opens the door. 

We enter a room from hell. It appears to be a torture chamber. There is a human male and a tiefling female, both shackled to a wall. On the table is a potted plant. The mummy animates. 

The mummy swings at [b]Turin[/b]. [b]Turin[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] both attack and do damage. [b]Arada[/b] leaps on it and attacks, plunging a dagger in its neck. 

[b]Martin[/b] heals the two people in stocks while [b]Arada[/b] releases them. The woman is very freaked. Lena and Tatum are the names of the couple. Lena was kidnapped and Tatum tried and failed to rescue her. They say the cultists have taken over the church.  It has all been women that they have seen. We offer drinks. [b]Arada[/b] examines the chest which contains a small amount of money.

We take them back to the main room and [b]Martin[/b] stays with them, while the rest of the party examines the last door. [b]Arada[/b] checks the door and the group goes into a brief hallway which ends at a secret door that is locked. [b]Arada[/b] checks for traps and unlocks the door.  

They find a small office with a desk. On the desk are a silver mark, five dice and a sheet of paper and quill. The paper has two columns with a symbol at the top of each. Other than that there are old robes and nothing otherwise exciting. The door leads outside. [b]Penelope[/b] looks where there ought to be a space. The group thinks they have seen the church and side building. 

Lena and Tatum ask to be escorted back to Killwitch. The whole area is still incredibly foggy. As we start to walk away, we hear a sound.  In the mist we see an evil death unicorn which runs away.

We make it back to town uneventfully. Lena has a touching reunion with her brother, and there is a happy family get together. He gives us a pouch containing 50 gold crowns, which we return. Instead we ask for room and board. He writes us a long, and poorly written letter that explains how awesome we are.

[h2]On the Road Again[/h2]
The next day we head back to Haven. On our way back, we are accosted by a man and a woman who ask if we are headed to Haven. They are Manykeor and Sarah, the head of Oakenville. They have a goblin problem and all their townfolk are off at Timberfloatn. While we discuss what to do, Turin follows them to help.

[h2]Oakenville, Sweet Oakenville[/h2]
We spend the rest of the day on the road, headed south and camp rested and refreshed. As we trundle along, we come to a clearing with three goblins accosting a lumberjack. Eugene splats one, while Martin Sanctuaries the lumberjack. Turin kills another, and we kill the third.

We get to Oakenville.  
[hr][img:6569583|nolink][hr]
It is suggested we take Yuri to Elenya Silverbraid at the Temple. She is a half-elf priestess of Lohi. She starts fussing over him, She heals him, and takes us to the Inn.  During the walk, we confirm the temple is open to all faiths, and she would be thrilled to have a wedding ceremony held at her temple.

Looking around the inn we see an assortment of townsfolk. Jocasta lets us in the private room to talk with the leaders of the town. Mandyk, Bartholomeo (halfling) the village merchant,  Masha the herbalist (tiefling). There have always been goblins, but lately it has gotten worse. After a storm two months ago, many trees got knocked down and the goblins started coming out in force. The trees all seem to be pointed to a central spot.

Masha gives us some healing potions for our journey. Yuri joins us. They fell towards a common point, even though they were all over the forest. Which just happens to be the new clearing. The goblins are coming from all over. 

We decide to get some sleep and explore in the morning. We also requested a map to the clearing.

articles.title: Session 17
articles.content:
[h2]Why is There a Trap Door Here?[/h2]
The next morning …

Yuri brings a map and gives it to [b][b]Turin[/b][/b]. Basically follow the path east for a while, then … well follow the map.  It should take 2-3 hours.

We follow the path east, with [b]Turin[/b] in the front. At a fork a pig emerges from a clearing. We hear a voice saying “and stay away from my house!”  A pixie emerges from the woods. [b]Martin[/b] says hello to the pixie. They commiserate about the big folks. [b]Martin[/b] confirms she polymorphed the lumberjack because he was relieving himself on her house.

The pixie unpolymorphs the pig turning him back into a dwarven lumberjack. The lumberjack is deeply apologetic and he heads back to Oakenville. [b]Martin[/b] offers that if she marks her house, he will tell the townsfolk to avoid it.

After a bit, we come to a clearing. In the middle is a woodshed, probably set up by the lumberjacks. There are a couple piles of lumber. We think this is the clearing. [b]Turin[/b] sees many tracks, including some goblin coming in and out of the shed. He also hears rustling from the woods.

[b]Turin[/b] slips into the woods while we continue to the ambush site. As we advance, [b]Turin[/b] finds a lone goblin and dispatches it. He steps out of the woods with a thumbs up and we hear three more goblins scream. We engage the goblins to mixed results. They are surprisingly resilient. Or we are bad at aiming.  One of those two.

We go into the shed, there is a cot and minor storage.  And a trap door. A trapped trap door. [b]Arada[/b] discovers a trip wire connected to a dangerous vial. [b]Eugene[/b] mage hands it down and gives it to [b]Arada[/b]. She squees at the sign of it.

[b]Penelope[/b] opens the trap door and we descend a rickety ladder.  A corridor leads west. There are lots of goblin tracks. The main path leads west with a small corridor north. The tunnel leads to a much more finished hallway. 

There are many goblins to the north and the south. They engage and do some damage to [b]Martin[/b]. [b]Turin[/b] kills one and hears a whisper in his head. [b]Penelope[/b] also kills one, and eventually she and [b]Arada[/b] finish them off.

A half-ogre? emerges from a door to the south asking who is disturbing its sleep. 

[b]Eugene[/b] hits Gurg (the half ogre?) with a magic missile and Gurg asks why you hit. [b]Martin[/b] heals Gurg (and himself) and convinces Gurg to not hurt us. Gurg says the goblins are further west.

The goblin bodies have insignias from two different tribes (three including the ones from earlier). [b]Arada[/b] with [b]Penelope[/b]’s help wedges the door as best and quietly as they can.

To the west are three doors, a set of double doors and a smaller door to the left. The left door leads to a walkway around a bigger room. There is a goblin and kobold leader with a retenue of kobolds. In front of the leaders is a spectral figure that sees us and orders them to kill the intruders. The kobolds leave through the north door. 

Combat begins. [b]Eugene[/b] targets the goblin boss who throws a javelin at [b]Turin[/b]. [b]Penelope[/b] smacks a goblin who has the audacity to miss her with an attack. [b]Turin[/b] hits the boss with his bow, but somehow the boss redirects the damage to another goblin. [b]Turin[/b] downs the boss, and we eventually kill the rest of the goblins. Eventually. We don’t talk about the last goblin. When [b]Turin[/b] downs the boss goblin, he hears a voice in his head saying "Well done son, I knew you could do it" Or something like that. He replies in his head "thanks dad" the voice responds "Since when do you call me dad?" He also tells him "as long as you possess my bow, we can have limited conversation." They chat for a while.

The bodies have two different tribes represented (five total) (plus the kobolds who peaced out). The boss had no insignia, but did have a javelin and scimitar. The boss also had a key (as bosses do). Under the throne we find a small box. Which the key opens. The box contains two scrolls and a small bag of gems/jewels. The two scrolls are “[i]False Life[/i]” and “[i]Identify[/i]”.

articles.title: Session 18
articles.content:
[h2]Goblins and Kobolds and Gurgs, oh my...[/h2]
[b]Penelope[/b] tries to lift the portcullis, but fails. She asks for help from [b]Turin[/b]. It is still firmly stuck.  [b]Arada[/b] finds a lock, but it doesn’t unlock. We decide to go check the far end of the other hallway.

An opening leads to the east, into an irregular cave with a walkway and a bridge over a chasm. There are thick webs all over the room. There are webbed shapes in the northwest corner. In the southwest corner are some large opalescent spider eggs. Looking up we see…nothing.  JK, it is a big spider.

The large spider drops down and tries to bite [b]Turin[/b] but misses. [b]Turin[/b] swings back and does a good chunk of damage.  He runs across the bridge which holds his weight. [b]Martin[/b] brings in light and [b]Arada[/b] attacks it, killing it. 

We carefully cross the bridge. [b]Penelope[/b] goes last, and the bridge breaks under her. She barely makes it to the other edge, [b]Turin[/b] helping her up. Unfortunately the passage is collapsed with a skeleton at the pile. The skeleton’s chain mail is rusty, but the cloak seems almost new.  As [b]Eugene[/b] reaches for the cloak, the skeleton attacks. 

[b]Arada[/b] misses it, but [b]Turin[/b] hits with an arrow. [b]Penelope[/b] uses her combat expertise to move [b]Eugene[/b] out of the way (Bait and Switch FTW). [b]Eugene[/b] kills it with his last spell (Magic Missile FTW).  [b]Eugene[/b] tries the cloak on, and finds it is a cloak of protection. Probably.

 We lower [b]Arada[/b] to the ground, and she touches down after 25 feet. After much discussion, we climb down. [b]Martin[/b] tries to check the bodies, which have been liquified. We exit through the southern passage, and [b]Eugene[/b] torches the place on the way out, including a small swarm of spiderlings.

We take a short rest in the boss room. [b]Arada[/b] unwedges Gurg’s door, and [b]Martin[/b] knocks on the door to wake Gurg up. Nobody stirs, so we go in. [b]Martin[/b] tries to wake Gurg and fails, but [b]Arada[/b] wakes him. Gurg wants to know what is happening, and [b]Martin[/b] convinces him that there is an evacuation and he needs to open the portcullis. 

Gurg rips the portcullis off the frame, drinks the contents of the cauldron down, and goes back to go to bed. After a short distance the tunnel is collapsed, which looks fairly recent. 

A voice booms: There’s nothing else here.  Go back to the inn.

So we get back to the inn, mid-evening or so.

We explain to the townsfolk about the dungeons, the kobolds, and the spectre. We killed the goblins, but the kobold leader (Kirrich) and their band are still out there. [b]Eugene[/b] shows them the goblin insignias, which are from all over, including the tribe that attacked [b]Turin[/b]’s village. [b]Turin[/b] knows it is rare for this many tribes to cooperate. 

We also mention the pixie (Lalalna) and they are moderately mortified.

[b]Turin[/b] is sure that with the spirit, something is going on. 


[h2]Ah, that's where the kobolds went[/h2]
The night passes and the next morning we ready ourselves to go home.

On the ride home, [b]Turin[/b] who was in front notices in the distance a group of creatures that look like kobolds. They are near what looks like a demoncorn. 

We sneak into position, and [b]Turin[/b] launches a Hail of Thorns. It does significant damage to the group of the kobolds, killing several.  [b]Martin[/b] creates a spiritual weapon killing another.

The boss misses [b]Martin[/b] with a bow shot, as [b]Eugene[/b] had foreseen. The undead unicorn flees, and a hidden kobold hits [b]Penelope[/b]. [b]Arada[/b] dispatches it with extreme lethality. [b]Penelope[/b] rushes up. Several kobolds hit her with their slings, but she kills one in retribution. [b]Eugene[/b] uses a magic missile to kill two more. Six seconds in and we’ve managed to uneven the odds.

[b]Turin[/b] kills the boss kobold and hears a voice in his head “Now would be a good time”. An arrow out of nowhere kills another kobold. 

[b]Martin[/b] heals [b]Penelope[/b] and [b]Arada[/b] sticks the one with her rapier, hitting it in the “explode here” spot. The last two kobolds make a valiant stand, that is very ineffective. [b]Eugene[/b] sleeps the last one so we can interrogate it.

The kobolds had crap on them, except for the boss who had a scimitar, javelin, chain shirt, and shield. I.e. slightly better crap.

[b]Martin[/b] heals [b]Arada[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] as we interrogate the kobold.

articles.title: Session 19
articles.content:
[h2]Gurbo's bad day[/h2]
[b]Eugene[/b] tries to wake the kobold up, eventually doing so.  We now have an awake terrified kobold. His name is Gurbo. 
[ul][li]How many of you are there?  Just me now.

[li]How many were there? Many.

[li]Are there more many groups like you? Yes, all over world. We take over some day?

[li]What was the undead horse? Scary undead horse zombie unicorn.

[li]Did it talk to you? No, chief talked to it.

[li]Did it talk in a language chief understood? Dunno, I didn’t understand.

[li]Was it mean conversation? No

[li]When did you first see it? Just before we showed up.

[li]Did anyone in your group know about it? Some did.

[li]Was this the first time you interacted with it? First time I did.

[li]What were you trying to do when you attacked it? Dunno, boss knew.  Scary ghost guy told boss, boss knew.

[li]Were you attacking it? No, you (party) attacked first.

[li]What can you tell us about scary ghost guy? He’s not solid.

[li]Can you understand? Yes, speaks common.

[li]What did it tell you? Nothing. It talk to chief.

[li]Did you overhear anything? <What we heard in boss room>

[li]Where is your camp? Were camping with goblins, now on our own.

[li]What will you do next? Depends on what you do.

[li]If we let you go, what will you do? Find more kobolds.
[/ul]

While the interrogation goes nowhere, [b]Turin[/b] looks for zombie hoofprints. There are some leading north.

We head back to the wagon, with [b]Turin[/b] lingering behind for … reasons.

We head back to Haven…


[h2]We have to go back Martin ...[/h2]
…  It is late in the day on the 15th.  The sun is well on its way to closing, the main portion of the gate is closed.  As we approach the city, a few hundred yards away.  We feel a tremendous lurch.  Everybody except [b]Penelope[/b] is bent over lurching, our heads pounding.  [b]Turin[/b]’s horse throws him to the ground. The cart horse falls to the ground, unable to get up.  

After a couple minutes, the pain starts to ease, our stomachs empty. We look around, and think we are in the same place at the same time. We look at the animals.

[b]Eugene[/b] looks at the contents of the cart to see if there is evidence of time passage. There isn’t.

A little out from the gate there are two women, one tall and one short.  The short one has her hands on her hips. As we get closer, the tall one is Lucille rilosa, and the short one is @[Jillie Ferndew](person:6f0f4057-ddb7-4306-a3df-72a4e1964b51) . Lucille was sent by @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) to watch for us.  

Jillie is pissed off.  [b]We’ve been gone for 4 weeks[/b] (so it's really 6th of Wealsun?!?).  She thinks we’ve abandoned our post. [b]Martin[/b] is flummoxed.  


[h2]... Back to the Future[/h2]
On our way in, we tell Lucille what has been going on.  

Apparently a few days ago the whole town shook and a massive tower appeared in Guilder’s Park.  It is taller than anything in the area.

People have apparently tried to get in, but there are traps around the door.  The watch as tried, but as soon as they started taking injuries they stopped. Even the thieves have been reluctant to try. 

Oh, and some people in @[The Labyrinth](settlement:e05f9a94-a44d-4991-b419-28cae89db0ee) people have been reporting two foot tall dolls have been wandering around. There is a creepy alley there.

We get back to the Gryphon.  @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) wants to know where we’ve been.  We tell her about the time jump.

We tell her about the death cult church, zombie unicorn. On our way back we received a report about a town under attack by goblins and kobolds. Yes, we should have called the militia, but we made a call.

We give @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) the note from the church town, which helps a little.

She orders us to go to the tower and investigate immediately.  After a brief clean and restock, we do so.
[hr][img:6571255|nolink][hr]
The tower has square pillars around it.  There are doors on the north and south walls, and two sets of doors on the east.

There are gears and whirrs. The doors are covered with intricate gears and knobs. [b]Arada[/b] investigates the door and finds nothing.  [b]Eugene[/b] casts detect magic, and is overwhelmed by the amount of magic.  It is all magic.  All of it. Mostly enchantment and transmutation.  And a third unknown magic.

[b]Arada[/b] searches for traps, and hears a faint hissing sound and some moisture near a pipe. She guides [b]Eugene[/b] using his mage hand to poke near the hissing. There is a blast of steam that goes out five feet or so.  There is the same effect from the other door, along with a few other pipes. [b]Arada[/b] works on disabling the traps.

[b]Arada[/b] unlocks the door and opens it.  She sees a dark tunnel leading straight ahead, going in at least ten feet. 

We come to a spot with a translucent shimmer in the air.  We proceed through it. [b]Arada[/b]’s torch goes through it and vanishes. [b]Penelope[/b] ties a rope around [b]Martin[/b] and he walks through. They confirm he can go back through. 

We come to a door. Through it is a room with every contraption, frozen in place.  There is a giant timepiece that is silent

We see two clockwork birds that are weaving in and out of each other, their wings a combination of feathers and metal.  On the floor is the face of a clock, the hands of which are just above the floor.

The birds give us a riddle:

[container:indent-20][b]Tick:[/b] “Chirp-chirp! The L-Lobby door, where time does snore! Needs p-p-pieces from junkyard floor!"
[p][b]Tock:[/b] "Tock-tock! In the j-j-junkyard lore, find what the clock does implore!"[/container]

[b]Martin[/b] repairs a timepiece with a watchglass, [b]Penelope[/b] goes to oil the clock with worn out gears. There are pieces missing. [b]Martin[/b] finds a plant.

[b]Eugene[/b] tries in vain to find a way under the floor.  We fix some clocks.

Eventually we go through one of the exits, there is a hallway similar to the one that got us here.

[b]Martin[/b] walks down the tunnel and there is a shimmering wall. He goes through. The party comes along. There is a set of stairs leading up.  We come to a doorway and come up into a room of fragmented mirrors, each reflecting distorted views.  A distorted image is visible
[hr][img:6571271|nolink][hr]
[aloud]The ethereal figure of Constantine forms in the shimmering reflection of the newly restored mirrors. His translucent visage contorts with a mix of relief and despair.   "Adventurers," he begins, his voice echoing through the chamber with a hollow resonance, "my Clockwork Tower, a marvel of time and engineering, now stands on the precipice of collapse. I need your aid to right my wrongs.  

"In my zeal and ambition, I left much undone. As you ascend, you will find my research, incomplete designs, and abandoned constructs. The time control device, my magnum opus, remains unfinished, a testament to my hubris. Seek the blueprint, lost in the maze of gears and pendulums. Only by completing my work can you restore harmony to the tower's temporal core.  

"Time, while a fascinating subject of study, is an unforgiving master. The tower groans and grinds, its once seamless operations marred by decay and neglect. Every jammed door, every grinding gear, is a cry for help.

"The very heart of the tower flutters erratically, like a bird trapped in a cage. Power surges and wanes, causing rooms to vanish, constructs to go awry, and time itself to warp." His voice grows desperate, "You must find an alternative source of power or stabilize the existing source."  

He looks at you, hope gleaming in his spectral eyes, "Help me set things right. Restore the Clockwork Tower to its former glory. Only then can I find peace, and the tower continue its journey."
[/aloud]

[container:indent-20]
[b]Tick:[/b] "Chirp-chirp! Echoes broken, mirrors lost!  Mayor Elspeth knows the cost!"
[br][b]Tock:[/b] "Tock-tock!  Phantoms of P-p-paradox play, in Council Chambers, causing disarray!"


[b]Tick:[/b] "Chirp-chirp! Constantine's s-s-song, trapped in time, all along!"
[br][b]Tock:[/b] "Tock-tock! Wise yet wild, the inventor's spirit, time's lost child!"
[/container]

We take the other exit.  Surprise! Another tunnel and shimmery wall.

We go to a dilapidated hallway, more mechanical spiders. To our left is a small platform with colored plinths, and multiple lines along the floor

[container:indent-20]
[b]Tick: [/b]"Chirp-chirp! Cogsprocket Sp-sp-spiders lurk, guarding knowledge, their tireless work!"
[br][b]Tock:[/b] "Tock-tock! Mind where you s-s-step, or in spiders' web, you might get swept!"
[/container]

In the middle of the room is a swirling energy field. [b]Eugene[/b] goes to read the book, and gets a shock. We find a small amount of stuff.  

[b]Martin[/b] gets caught in a book tornado as [b]Penelope[/b] discovers she can move the plinths…

articles.title: Session 2
articles.content:
The second day of our 1st assignment! Go patrol @[The Labyrinth](settlement:e05f9a94-a44d-4991-b419-28cae89db0ee) 

Past Plaza of Troubadors then to Fogelberry Street

Note: No brothels

Fogelberry and Cross St. We have an adventure

There is a wolf attacking a small boy. We are being heroic . 
[ul]
[li][b]Martin[/b] cast shield of faith on the boy and [b]Penelope[/b]. The wolf attacked [b]Penelope[/b] and hurt her. Then [b]Arada[/b] hit it with her shortbow and did a lot of damage. [b]Turin[/b] finished off the wolf. Then the boy laughs at us and says we are funny. 
[li]A giant rat appears. 
[li][b]Penelope[/b] tackled the kid, [b]Turin[/b] and [b]Martin[/b] chased a thief that was yelled out by the crowd. They chased him to the Thieves market. 
[li]Meanwhile, [b]Penelope[/b], [b]Arada[/b], and [b]Eugene[/b] discussed the appropriate level of corporal punishment. Two local guards appeared. One @[Lucille Rilossa](person:ce375714-67ed-4fad-810b-bc3b0eaf0fad) recognized the boy as Cal. 
[li][b]Turin[/b] and [b]Martin[/b] chase the thief, over roofs to the theives market.[b] Turin[/b] jumps off a building Bad Boys style. Eventually we herd him back to the party and arrest him. 
[li]We turned him into Lucille. She is interested in our unit.
[/ul]

articles.title: Session 20
articles.content:
[h2]The Tower[/h2]
We look at the plinth.  We recognize four of the six - The Sun, Antheros (the planet), Hasp and Celene (the two moons).  The other two are broken.  [b]Eugene[/b] recalls from university days there were some non twinkling stars. 

[b]Martin[/b] has a piece that might go on one of the plinths, but can’t figure out how they fit. [b]Arada[/b] figures it out, and it completes the symbol for Tabit.  Tabit is a maybe star (i.e. planet). [b]Martin[/b] mends it just in case.

One of [b]Eugene[/b]’s books also lists clockworks, and also shows clockwork spiders of many types. And a guardian thingy.

With some divine help, [b]Martin[/b] finds Part E. It completes a symbol for Ebla (another planet). As soon as the last plinth was mended, they all moved to a new position. 

[b]Penelope[/b] tries to move a plinth. They seem to be locked in place. 

We leave through the north west door, leading to another hallway and shimmering passage. We end up at the southeast door of the main room. 

We try the northeast door.  It leads to another workroom.  
[hr][img:6571393|center|nolink][hr]
[container:indent-20][b]Tick:[/b] "Chirp-chirp! O-O-Olivia knows, how to fix, where it goes!"
[br]
[b]Tock:[/b] "Tock-tock! Sprites play tricks, on the way to Olivia's mix!"[/container]

One workbench is different and has an array of ornamental cogs.  There is a plaque on it:

[center]"Five circles tell of time and tower,[br]Use what's around, unlock the power.[br]Die casts its fate, a coin does gleam,[br]Cup holds a secret, cards tell a dream."[/center]

[b]Penelope[/b] recalls there are four elements, four directions, the dice reads four, four cards (tower, flame, cloud, rock), the coin is reflective.  [b]Penelope[/b] also notes the symbols could represent the progress of civilization. The fifth circle has an engraving of a clock face. The cup is a wooden cup. [b]Turin[/b] recalls water symbolizes life or barrier (like a moat). [b]Penelope[/b] pours some water in the cup. [b]Martin[/b] puts a watch in the middle.

We try putting the dice, coin, cup, and cards in the four spots. When we turn the cup upside down, we hear a click.  A drawer under the table opens. Inside the drawer is an ornate bronze medallion fea[b]Turin[/b]g gears and cogs. We also find a folded piece of paper. 

[container:indent-40]"To the one who understands the rhythm of the cogs and the dance of time, wear this medallion with pride. For you are now a guardian of this tower's legacy.[br]- C. Chronosmith"[/container]

[b]Eugene[/b] identifies the medallion. It is the Clockworkers Medallion. 

We go through the north door.

We find the forge room. In the middle of the room is a construct trying to fuel the forge (without success). 

[b]Arada[/b] approaches @[the construct](person:7267ca0f-4f12-4940-b565-5799dc7901b0).  It goes beep beep.  [b]Arada[/b] names him beep beep.

[b]Martin[/b] starts aligning the belt. It runs more smoothly. [b]Turin[/b] finds something jammed in the gear and tries to pull it free. As it starts running he pulls his fingers free.  Beep beep says beep beep beep. [b]Turin[/b] and [b]Martin[/b] apply oil and elbow grease to clean the rusty spool.  It also starts turning.

[b]Penelope[/b] cleans the grate that is rusted shut. Beep beep opens it.

Beep Beep says it needs: 
[ul][li]@[Ironwood Coal](material:4bbbbf5a-8a88-4be1-bbe2-9c1781cf2c71), [li]@[Earthheart Emerald](material:63f8cdd2-8d51-4846-996e-19525db18f7d)[li]@[Haven Steel](material:d3e332b3-51f9-4013-ae98-611d3b0d36c6) [/ul]

We will have to go outside the tower to get it. 

We head out the northwest door, which leads to the northwest door of the lobby.

We go to the stairs at the top of the stage.

As we enter the grand hall, and are in an Escher painting of time.

[hr][img:6571391|center|nolink][hr]

In the middle is a pedestal. Towering above the pedestal is another [spoiler][block:1409607]|large construct.[/spoiler]

The construct slams its staff into the ground and the room shakes.

The construct is angry.  A staff slam injuries all of us (except [b]Arada[/b]) and knocks [b]Eugene[/b] unconscious. [b]Martin[/b] heals him, and we flee. 

We debate if we should leave and report back, or stay.  Eventually we think we should probably just leave. We end up going back to the inn.
