articles.title: Session 21
articles.content:
[h2]A brief interlude[/h2]
We go downstairs early evening.  @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) tells us the mayor wants to meet us early the next morning.  There are things going on, but they are trying to keep it tight.

It is too late for us to go to the university now or to source stuff.

Also the mages are swarming the tower by this point.


[h2]A vigorous debate in the council chambers[/h2]
Aside: The Haven Ironworks in in @[The Labyrinth](settlement:e05f9a94-a44d-4991-b419-28cae89db0ee) (U05)

We get up the next morning and head to @[Mayor's Palace](landmark:c0ab8179-cf4b-415f-b825-6c2535ae893b) (City Hall and the Council Chamber).
[hr][img:6088733|nolink][hr]
Sitting at a desk in the middle of the room is Mayor @[Elsbeth Windermere](person:b2466c19-d35e-45af-b070-831261248185). The air seems to vibrate and quiver. In the corner of our eyes we see shapes appear and disappear.

The tower has been here for seven days.  We explain that the tower was probably built partly in Haven.  Nobody knows of it directly.  Prof Gearhardt knows a little (he’s in the archives).

We tell her about the birds and how they mentioned the mayor by name.  Also that she knew the cost and there were phantoms of paradox.  As we say that, an apparition appears.

[b]Penelope[/b] goes to protect the mayor and [b]Turin[/b] shoots it with an arrow.  There is a shimmer, and we seem to see a human male at the podium arguing and pointing at a council member. The person being pointed to looks like Harz DeLaRiis, a confidant of @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de). 

[b]Arada[/b] shakes off the vision and stabs it. [b]Eugene[/b] and [b]Martin[/b] protect the mayor who withdraws.  The spirit attacks [b]Arada[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] finishes it off.

[b]Eugene[/b] asks what happens in the past. The mayor says one or two come through, hurt a few people, then stop. It started about a month ago.

[b]Eugene[/b] casts detect magic.  [b]Martin[/b] asks about the argument. The mayor says she thinks it was in the past, and was Michael Swiftsword, pointing at @[Harz de la Riis](person:35c67bc0-3762-429d-b35e-c4d0eac46490). The two of them aren’t normally chummy, but that isn’t unexpected.

[b]Eugene[/b] doesn’t see anything, but another phantom attacks him, and misses as he had foreseen. [b]Arada[/b] shoots it with her bow and misses. Another weird shift happens. The room goes different and a new scene appears. Swiftsword is at a chair, and Harz and @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) are at the podium. Harz seems to be congratulating her. Things shift back.  [b]Turin[/b] shoots it but misses and [b]Martin[/b] Sacred Flame it.

[b]Penelope[/b] rushes up and swaps places with [b]Eugene[/b]. [b]Eugene[/b] blasts it with magic missile and dissolves it. He continues his detect magic search.

[b]Martin[/b] mentions Olivia and Constantine.  The mayor thinks that Gearhardt mentioned an @[Olivia Chronosmith](person:17bc78d2-213b-481c-8a3b-f2dc64dafe23).  

[b]Turin[/b] asks about the @[Ironwood Coal](material:4bbbbf5a-8a88-4be1-bbe2-9c1781cf2c71), @[Earthheart Emerald](material:63f8cdd2-8d51-4846-996e-19525db18f7d), and @[Haven Steel](material:d3e332b3-51f9-4013-ae98-611d3b0d36c6). 

[b]Penelope[/b] notices a box of mirrors. The mayor says that they were going to be hung up in the council room.  

The mayor gets a page to take us to the archives to meet Gearhardt. The page takes to the archives and tells us to go upstairs and leaves.  We go upstairs.

As we enter the halls, a windburst strikes [b]Turin[/b].  [b]Martin[/b] calls out, and also hits a tornado.

We explore a bit. One of the rooms is a storeroom.  The other is Gearhardt’s office.

The @[professor](person:b1e28c32-ce9e-479c-8860-1cfc4ce08cc7) wants to know why we are in his office.  [b]Martin[/b] confuses him. We start discussing the tower and @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17).

He thinks the tower was built in the long past.  We mention Olivia, and he recognizes the name. He thinks Olivia is the last living Chronosmith descendent.  He has a book of Constantine’s notes.

We explore a bit and find a stair leading to not this adventure.  [b]Penelope[/b] finds an old patent telling us how to build beep beep. [b]Eugene[/b]’s reading of the notes suggests that we need a temporal resonator. 

We look around the library and find many interesting volumes, including sketches of the tower, schematics of defense systems, principles of time magic, and blueprints for a temporal matrix. [b]Eugene[/b] leaves some of the books with Gearhardt.

Gearhardt thinks Olivia might be a watchmaker.  There are a few streets where there are a lot of jewelry shops.

[b]Martin[/b] suggests we talk to the guards in that area and see if they know of her shop.

articles.title: Session 22
articles.content:
[h2]Olivia, Lyza, Lucille, and Coraline[/h2]
[h3]Early afternoon[/h3]

We head towards @[Caravan Station](landmark:04a3d0b6-0486-4c59-8809-ad679781cb5a). Along @[Gold Alley](landmark:126c954d-648d-488e-a7d5-5c9f436c95df), [b]Martin[/b] casts locate object to try to find a watchmaker’s vice.  He gets the group close to where he thinks one is, but the spell runs out.

Go to a nearby jewelry shop to ask if they know @[Olivia Chronosmith](person:17bc78d2-213b-481c-8a3b-f2dc64dafe23). The jeweler tells us that her shop is in an alley nearby.  We head there and find Olivia’s shop.  

We enter.  It is a cramped, but functional workshop.
[hr][img:6571915|center|nolink][hr]
We explain that we think she is connected to the tower and is the last descendent of the tower’s constructor.

She is a bit shocked, and stunned that family history is real.

She is also surprised that we got into the tower and survived.

She mentions a music box that is the key to the tower. Which she still has. [b]Martin[/b] remembers one of the books mentioned how the defense system was activated by music.

A flash comes out of the back room and makes a beeline toward [b]Penelope[/b].  Something small lands on her and starts tinkering with her armor. [b]Arada[/b] sees a one foot tall winged sprite.

Olivia winds the music box. We hear a haunting but soothing melody.

[b]Eugene[/b] shows her the medallion. We describe beep beep.  

We also describe the jumping in town, and she mentions that it sounds like wild chronology magic.

We describe what we need for the tower. She thinks @[The Foundry](landmark:bf4238e7-34eb-47da-a388-a1f4b9ff93ea) might have the steel and the @[Ironwood Coal](material:4bbbbf5a-8a88-4be1-bbe2-9c1781cf2c71).  She isn’t sure about the emerald(s).

She also mentions there is a junkyard to the west of town somewhere.

She is going to read over night and we’ll see her in the morning.

We head back to the inn to report … in.

We tell @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) what we learned with the mayor and council, Gearhart, and @[Olivia Chronosmith](person:17bc78d2-213b-481c-8a3b-f2dc64dafe23).

@[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) reminds us that Lucille rilosa is looking for some help with the murder dolls in @[The Labyrinth](settlement:e05f9a94-a44d-4991-b419-28cae89db0ee).

We scarf down dinner, and head to the @[Market Barracks](landmark:8b369554-047b-483f-8486-6460bd10c977). It is now 7:00

Lucille tells us the rumors are the dolls are between two and ten feet tall.  She says she’s heard all kinds of stories.  

She takes us to where the alley where things are reported.  She tells us to expect a creep sensation and possibly fog out of nowhere.

The cobblestone alley is eerily quiet. There is a chill wind, and the smell of decay. A mist begins to form.Down a side alley, we can see shapes that seem … wrong. The sounds of whispering comes from the alley.

The map changes to combat mode.
[hr][img:6571917|center|nolink] [hr]
A doll swings its doll at [b]Eugene[/b] misses badly. As he had foreseen. It also misses with its second attack. Another doll misses [b]Turin[/b].

Marionettes come at our feet. [b]Penelope[/b] switches places with [b]Eugene[/b] and smacks it with her sword. [b]Arada[/b] hops down and attacks the doll. 

[b]Martin[/b] bonds [b]Arada[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b], and [b]Eugene[/b] casts a web to slow the ones at the end of the alley.

[b]Turin[/b] shoots one of the ones at the end of the alley. 

A doll sings a creepy lullaby and frightens [b]Penelope[/b], [b]Turin[/b], and [b]Arada[/b]. [b]Martin[/b] tries to turn them, but it doesn’t work.  The far ones approach and try to attack [b]Turin[/b] who runs in fear.

[b]Penelope[/b] splats the one that was frightening [b]Arada[/b], and [b]Arada[/b] runs back to sneak attack one.

[b]Martin[/b] heals [b]Turin[/b] and is frightened by one of the dolls. [b]Turin[/b] critically hits the one in the middle causing it to collapse. [b]Penelope[/b] and [b]Arada[/b] shish kebab the third one, and [b]Eugene[/b] finishes it off with a fire bolt.

We quickly finish off the last one, peppering it with arrows, rapiers, sacred flames, and fire. 

As we kill the last one, the creepiness doesn’t go away.

One of the buildings is marked “toy shop”.  Inside we see a time capsule of the macabre. [b]Eugene[/b] detects magic. There is creepy glowing necromantic magic around us, though nothing specific.

We decide to lock it up and come back another day.  On our way out of @[The Labyrinth](settlement:e05f9a94-a44d-4991-b419-28cae89db0ee), we let Lucille know that we’re only half done.
 
We let @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) know, and go to sleep.

articles.title: Session 23
articles.content:
[h2]This is Halowe'en, everybody run and scream[/h2]
We level up overnight

The next morning…

We decide to go finish the doll situation and also go to the Haven Ironworks. We send an urchin to go to @[Olivia Chronosmith](person:17bc78d2-213b-481c-8a3b-f2dc64dafe23) and get him to bring Olivia back to the Griffon. [b]Martin[/b] offers 1 silver, 5 copper with a silver after Olivia comes there.

We go to the murder doll house alley. (Haven map O2)

When we get to the alley where the dolls were, the alley is dark and foggy. There is a dead body in the street.  [b]Martin[/b] runs up. It appears to be an elven woman. She is wearing ragged clothing and her throat is slit. Her abdomen is also slit open and entrails are spilling out.

[b]Arada[/b] sees small doll sized footprints.

We enter.

There are a collection of old toys. A locked chest is in the corner, a ladder leads to an attic.  We get a sense of something unfinished.
[hr][img:6572316][hr]
[b]Penelope[/b] starts collecting dolls while [b]Arada[/b] inspects the chest. The floor under the chest looks uneven. The floor under the chest has been tampered with and is hiding something below. [b]Arada[/b] ties the rope around the chest with Mage Hand help. [b]Penelope[/b] and [b]Turin[/b] drag it out of the corner. [b]Penelope[/b] rolls it over to look. 

[b]Arada[/b] opens the chest. Inside are more toys and toy parts.

[b]Turin[/b] pulls down the ladder. [b]Arada[/b] climbs the ladder and looks in. She sees a ton of toys scattered over the floor. And a creepy doll.  She runs up and hits the creepy doll and runs away. The creepy doll follows her down the ladder. The party engages it, but doesn’t quite get it down. It sings, fat lot of good that does.  

[b]Turin[/b] goes into the attic. It is full of junk. He roots around a little. Eventually he finds a sizeable mirror, roughly 2x3 feet tall with a fancy frame. The mirror has a curse on it to trap a soul. Once a soul is trapped, you can use it to transfer it to another vessel.  

In the journal, the author talks a bit about his daughter, but nothing about souls. [b]Eugene[/b] detects magic under the floor. It looks like the board might move. Pulling the boards back there is a ladder leading down to a workroom.

[b]Turin[/b] goes down. There is a deep sense of unease. In the center of the room is a broken mirror, with arcane symbols written on the floor. [b]Eugene[/b] comes down. The rest of us follow.

From the shadows, a figure steps forward. It is a life sized doll with delicate figures. Dolls come to life and a malevolent voice says “you abandoned him and left us all to suffer”

[b]Turin[/b] severely damages one of them.  [b]Eugene[/b] also magic missiles it.  [b]Penelope[/b] thwacks a creepy doll, and [b]Martin[/b] makes more light.  Marionette strings grapple [b]Eugene[/b] and [b]Turin[/b].

We are a bit spread out, slowly working on the dolls. 

The mirror in the middle of the room flares, and reflections of [b]Turin[/b], [b]Eugene[/b], and [b]Martin[/b] come flying out of the mirror. [b]Martin[/b] sees a vision of himself as a zombie reaching out and pokes his brain.

The dolls slowly attack us, and we attack back. [b]Turin[/b] blasts a significant barrage into the big doll.  [b]Eugene[/b] uses magic missile to kill one of the dolls, and to hurt the boss doll.  [b]Martin[/b] heals people with the high five line, and the boss moves to attack [b]Turin[/b].

The boss injures [b]Turin[/b], and [b]Arada[/b] hurts a doll. [b]Penelope[/b] finishes a doll and [b]Martin[/b] goes to heal [b]Turin[/b]. The mirror flares again, and hurts [b]Martin[/b] and [b]Arada[/b].  The mirror hates halflings. It’s racist.

[b]Turin[/b] hits the boss, but it does not die, though it seems less solid.  [b]Arada[/b] smashes the broken mirror more, but nothing happens. [b]Eugene[/b] kills the last creepy doll, leaving only the boss.

[b]Turin[/b] rushes upstairs to grab the other mirror. [b]Eugene[/b] takes it from him and sucks the soul of the boss doll into it.  The foreboding vanishes.

articles.title: Session 24
articles.content:
[h2]Hot time in the Foundry tonight...this afternoon[/h2]
We all stand around looking at a face down mirror. We take a bolt of cloth to wrap it around the mirror and discuss taking it to Lady Roweena Geronde who we met after dealing with her family crypt in @[Session 12](report:57e5c0aa-0053-442a-bae9-879cebb94825).

[b]Eugene[/b], [b]Turin[/b], and [b]Penelope[/b] discuss the best way to wrap and carry the mirror. [b]Arada[/b] and [b]Martin[/b] look around. [b]Martin[/b] finds a few interesting mechanical toys. [b]Arada[/b] finds a bag of clinky stuff. [b]Eugene[/b] does a last look around and finds a cryptex. 

We realize we ought to be back to meet Olivia soon, so we head to the Griffon, stopping briefly at the Haven Ironworks since it is on the way. As we approach the ironworks, it is a hive of industry, with activity on the inside.  Just inside there is a security dwarf who points us to Dahlia.

She says she can help us, if we get appropriate authorization. She also says asks if we can help her with some clockwork dogs that are wandering around and periodically attacking things and causing trouble. We agree to help in a sort of informal quid pro quo. Also some of her people are in there: Seraphina and Grimm.

We meet @[Seraphina Steamspark](person:16390999-39e0-4310-913a-308aec509a5d). She doesn’t know how many dogs there are.  She also offers to help with the tower, but she has lost her tools. 

[b]Arada[/b] opens a door and finds a dog.

There is also a dwarf in the room, keeping his distance.  [b]Turin[/b] steps in to take a shot at the dog. [b]Eugene[/b] hits it with a firebolt. The dog runs up and attempts to bite [b]Eugene[/b] but misses. [b]Arada[/b] finishes it off. 

Grimm gruffly thanks us and asks not to speak with [b]Martin[/b] any more. 

Grimm and Seraphina have a cutscene. 

The next room is very warm, so we go back to the other side of the entrance. [b]Penelope[/b] shouts “Look! Another dog!” Lo and behold there is another dog. [b]Arada[/b] runs up and misses it. [b]Turin[/b] attacks and hits. [b]Eugene[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] finish it off.

[b]Arada[/b] opens a door and shouts “Dog!” On command the dog decides to fetch. Specifically fetch [b]Penelope[/b]’s spleen. [b]Martin[/b] heals Penelops and tells the dog it is a bad dog. [b]Arada[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] both miss the dog badly. The dog also misses because it feels bad. [b]Turin[/b] thwacks it with an arrow. [b]Penelope[/b] kills a third clockwork dog.

Moving on we see another dog. There is also a half-elf dressed in office clothing cowering near the dog. In a cutscene, the dog kills the accountant (Lars Koppercoil). 

[b]Penelope[/b] engages and hits and kills the dog. [b]Martin[/b] heals the accountant and brings him back from the dead. Meanwhile [b]Eugene[/b] sees another dog. A worker is avoiding the dog. Korbin is a really, really tall goliath. As we rush in, there is yet another dog to the north. [b]Martin[/b] pokes the dog to the north with a spell. [b]Turin[/b] then explodes it with an arrow. [b]Penelope[/b] swings at hers and misses. Korbin hits it with a hammer fist and it crumbles. 

We continue looking for dogs and tools. And taking the accountant to the door. We decide to explore the east half of @[The Foundry](landmark:bf4238e7-34eb-47da-a388-a1f4b9ff93ea).  Yay, another dog!  It charges and bites at [b]Arada[/b]. But misses. [b]Penelope[/b] hurts it badly.  Do clockwork dogs feel pain? [b]Martin[/b] tickles it with a spell and [b]Turin[/b] finishes it. In a very hot room [b]Penelope[/b] and [b]Turin[/b] find a steel like ingot. @[Haven Steel](material:d3e332b3-51f9-4013-ae98-611d3b0d36c6) maybe?

[b]Turin[/b], [b]Eugene[/b], and [b]Penelope[/b] explore the back and find a few lumps of @[Ironwood Coal](material:4bbbbf5a-8a88-4be1-bbe2-9c1781cf2c71).

We think we’ve explored the whole place and go back to talk to Dahlia, Seraphina, Grimm, and the Accountant. [b]Martin[/b] gives Seraphina her tools. [b]Eugene[/b] asks about the ingot we found. It is unfinished @[Haven Steel](material:d3e332b3-51f9-4013-ae98-611d3b0d36c6), but if we get a letter from the mayor of city council we can get it.

[b]Martin[/b] also tells her if they see more to talk to their local watch to send us word.

We go back to the inn to report to @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de). Neither @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) nor Olivia are there.

articles.title: Session 25
articles.content:
[h2]I've had it with these Monday to Friday bodies in thie Melon Farmer tower[/h2]
[b][b]Eugene[/b][/b] goes to see if Thom can do a quick mend but there is no answer.

[b][b]Martin[/b][/b] asks @[Porky](person:e267fbb1-0d16-45d6-88c5-2bae4738a1dc) and Mrs Clever if they have seen @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) and they say not recently. 

We decide to go see how Olivia, the tower and/or Mayor are doing.

On the door of Olivia’s shop is a note that says “Gone Junk Picking, Back Whenever.” We leave the shop to stop by the Mayor’s palace to ask about our note. The clerk directs us to the second floor. We go to the Mayor’s office to ask. Many people are working outside. We leave a note for the mayor to authorize us to get some @[Haven Steel](material:d3e332b3-51f9-4013-ae98-611d3b0d36c6) from the Have Ironworks to fix the Haven Clockwork Tower of Doom.

<[b][b]Eugene[/b][/b] writes a note for the Mayor and gives it to Maurice, the Chief Clerk to the Mayor>

[b][b]Turin[/b][/b] checks to see if the Mayor is in, but doesn’t find anything. [b][b]Arada[/b][/b] thinks she recognizes one of the clerks (Chelsea). She hands her tabard and sneaks over. [b][b]Arada[/b][/b] confirms the mayor is out at some function. 

We trundle downstairs and walk to the tower fully expecting to see a group of guardsmen. But there aren’t any that we can see. There does appear to be rope tied around the pillars as a vague caution tape. There is an empty chair and a bottle and some cigarette butts. 

[b][b]Eugene[/b][/b] asks a passerby if they know where the guard are. The passenger says they were there earlier today. [b][b]Turin[/b][/b] sees a black handprint beside the open door.  [b][b]Eugene[/b][/b], [b][b]Penelope[/b][/b] and [b][b]Arada[/b][/b] go to Noble Way Station and see if they know why the guard aren’t there. [b][b]Martin[/b][/b] and [b][b]Turin[/b][/b] stay to keep an eye out inconspicuously. The sergeant is stunned to hear the crew isn’t there. He says he is going to send a squad and come look too.

Meanwhile [b][b]Turin[/b][/b] sits in the chair and [b][b]Martin[/b][/b] goes to look and see if anyone is keeping an eye. [b][b]Martin[/b][/b] sees a dwarf to the north leaning against the pillar to the north. [b][b]Martin[/b][/b] asks what the dwarf is doing and he says he is waiting for a friend. [b][b]Martin[/b][/b] wants to know what friend and why he is there. The dwarf is not cooperative. He is wearing a chestplate. A female tiefling comes up and asks if the copper is causing problems. [b][b]Martin[/b][/b] explains that the watch is trying to keep people from getting slaughtered inside. And lots of entrails. 

Eventually the party returns. They see a female tiefling against the pillar. [b][b]Martin[/b][/b] is pretty sure it is the same woman. He tells [b][b]Arada[/b][/b], [b][b]Penelope[/b][/b], and [b][b]Eugene[/b][/b] about the dwarf and tiefling. The sergeant comes back with a troop of men. They will keep guard while we go in.

We go in. There are blood stains and dead bodies. A human male with a guard tabard and human female in dark colors. [b][b]Turin[/b][/b] can tell both bodies have lots of little bites, as if they were bitten by large spiders. We haul the bodies back outside and give them to the sergeant.

@[Tick and Tock](person:98040fff-4014-41dc-b234-0f005a4e944f) say there were two in the tower and both killed by the spiders. And we are friends to the tower. So we have that going for us. We decide to have a quick look around. We tie a rope around [b][b]Martin[/b][/b] and [b][b]Penelope[/b][/b] and [b][b]Turin[/b][/b] and hold him while he peeks in at the death robot. No bodies, so yay. A quick look around the tower shows nothing else either.

[b][b]Turin[/b][/b] looks for guard footprints. In the southwest corner he sees a spot where the guard footprints turn to drag marks. The marks lead to the path north of Guilder’s Park. There may be a couple sets of footprints. The tracks lead towards the @[Guildhall of Provenders](landmark:c683418a-066a-4ab2-8f8f-746fecb502a9). [b][b]Turin[/b][/b] finds some additional drag marks leading to where the body might have gotten picked up. 

We let the sergeant know what we found. He has another set of guards. The missing guards will take a large dent out of their complement. The sergeant is going to talk to the officers and come up with a new plan for securing it.

We head back to the inn via Olivia’s but the note was still there. We return to the inn. @[Porky](person:e267fbb1-0d16-45d6-88c5-2bae4738a1dc) says No @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) but a fancy pants came with a scroll case. Based on the markings it appears to be official city business. We open it and find a piece of parchment on official city letterhead authorizing us to obtain reasonable amounts of @[Haven Steel](material:d3e332b3-51f9-4013-ae98-611d3b0d36c6) and @[Ironwood Coal](material:4bbbbf5a-8a88-4be1-bbe2-9c1781cf2c71). Signed by @[Elsbeth Windermere](person:b2466c19-d35e-45af-b070-831261248185). 

It is late, so we decide to go to sleep.


[h2]We're off to see a wizard[/h2]
@[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) still hasn’t come back. We’re sure she is fine.

We head to the Ironworks to meet with Dahlia. We ask for two bars and a couple bags of coal. At least as a reasonable start.  We bring Seraphina with us.

We arrive at     @[Olivia’s](plot:3d4f5d6a-d60f-4f59-97a2-c989bfc88849) and the note is still there. [b]Eugene[/b] gives @[Seraphina](person:16390999-39e0-4310-913a-308aec509a5d) a bag of clockwork dog parts to keep her busy, while we head to try to find the junkyard. Seraphina is going to the tower to wait for us there. 

We head to the junkyard. There is a fence and gate, but it is a giant yard of mechanical and rusted junk. It is supposed to be garbage, but it seems different. [b]Turin[/b] wanders off and encounters a ghosty rusty humanoid thing.

It almost seems to [b]Turin[/b] that it is trying to fix something. [b]Turin[/b] calls out to it, but it does not respond. The rest of us catch up. The rust engineer reaches out to touch [b]Penelope[/b]. It hurts.

[b]Turin[/b] shoots it and does serious damage. [b]Arada[/b] makes it go splat. 

We explore some more and see another one. [b]Turin[/b] shoots it and in response it charges [b]Turin[/b] but misses. [b]Martin[/b] rushes up and sees an unconscious Olivia. He sends a Healing Word at her. She is alive but unconscious.

Meanwhile [b]Eugene[/b] finds some clock parts. [b]Penelope[/b] also finds a spring. [b]Penelope[/b] and [b]Turin[/b] help Olivia to her feet. She remarks how both are big and strong.

[b]Eugene[/b] ritually casts detect magic. There is faint magic all around, probably chronology.

articles.title: Session 26
articles.content:
[h2]... the wonderful wizard of clock.[/h2]
We go to continue the exploration of the junkyard. A cloud of spores hits [b]Eugene[/b] and he temporarily loses the ability to speak. 

@[Olivia](person:17bc78d2-213b-481c-8a3b-f2dc64dafe23)  says she waited at the inn then left. She was exploring here then got knocked out. 

We continue exploring and find another clockwork dude and make short work of it. We explore and find clock parts.And another thing that [b]Turin[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] kill.

[b]Arada[/b] finds some shiny baubles and another bad guy around the corner. This one seems tougher than the others but we still make short work of it.
[hr][img:6572385|center|nolink][hr]
We look at all the clock stuff and decide to go back to the tower. @[Seraphina](person:16390999-39e0-4310-913a-308aec509a5d) is still there playing with the mechanical parts.  She has built a foot and a half tall humanoid. Seraphina and Olivia seem to hit it off very well. [b]Turin[/b] carries the robot.

We take Olivia and Seraphina to the tower lobby. @[Tick and Tock](person:98040fff-4014-41dc-b234-0f005a4e944f) say they will be fine if they don’t break the rules.  We realize we don’t actually know what the rules are. Oh well, that can’t come back to bite us in the butt.

Olivia and Seraphina go to open the clock door. @[Tick and Tock](person:98040fff-4014-41dc-b234-0f005a4e944f) mention the tower works on intent. We give the parts to the nerds and they work on fixing the clock. [b]Eugene[/b] notices a mechanical spider on the clock. We find it in the book of defenses called a Cogsprocket Weaver. 

[b]Martin[/b] and [b]Turin[/b] work on fixing some of the various odds and bobs on the workbench to get as many of them working as possible. We set the clocks to match the dial on the floor. 

[b]Penelope[/b] keeps guard on Seraphina while [b]Eugene[/b] tries to help. [b]Arada[/b] also works on unbending a bent gear. She puts it back but uses too much oil. Fortunately that is an easy fix. 

They manage to repair the clock and have it ready to be wound. Olivia inserts the crank and starts winding it. She removes the crank and we hear the tick of the clock moving and the minute hand slowly moving.

We offer to take Olivia and Seraphina to meet Beep Beep.  We give Beep Beep the steel and coal. He tosses the coal into the forge. We ask if he needs us to start a fire. He says yes, so [b]Eugene[/b] uses fire bolt to try to start one. The forge is lit.

 We mention the @[Earthheart Emerald](material:63f8cdd2-8d51-4846-996e-19525db18f7d), Olivia says those are old green stones that can splinter to make an edge, but doesn’t know where we could find one.

Beep Beep puts the @[Haven Steel](material:d3e332b3-51f9-4013-ae98-611d3b0d36c6) into the forge and it slowly melts and flows into a sand cast form just filling it.   We interrogate Beep Beep about what he’s making. It is something to fix the tower, but not for us to use. We learn it has enough steel and we aren’t supposed to use it. 

Beep Beep settles in to wait. Seraphina starts tinkering with Beep Beep. She and Olivia think they could add a voice box with some effort. 

It is getting late, but we take them to the Assembly Room on the way out. 
 
Before we leave, we have [b]Eugene[/b] take a peek at the angry construct using Sanctuary and his Clockworker’s Medallion.  The construct moves to attack then stops, looking at [b]Eugene[/b] … quizzically? 
[hr][img:6572399|center|nolink][hr]
We join him, leaving Seraphina and Olivia behind. Nothing happens so we bring Olivia and Seraphina. The two of them are amazed. 

@[Tick and Tock](person:98040fff-4014-41dc-b234-0f005a4e944f) join us. They fly around and through the moving pieces. 

[b]Tick:[/b] "Chirp-chirp! The p-p-pedestal's hollow call, seeks a crystal, to rise, not fall!"[br]
[b]Tock:[/b] "Tock-tock! @[Luminous Caverns](location:db5b92b1-7c72-4bf9-8424-38f27ba0f5aa) gleam, but Pendulum Horrors lurk and scheme!

Olivia thinks she heard of the @[Luminous Caverns](location:db5b92b1-7c72-4bf9-8424-38f27ba0f5aa) in the @[Khuz Mountains](location:806c0e95-17cf-4c38-9d20-c4cbfce74d4d), but nobody knows where they are. The caverns, not the mountains. The mountains are about a hundred miles away.

[hr][img:6572407|center|nolink][hr]

Olivia and Seraphina go to Olivia’s shop and we return to the Griffon mid-evening. @[Porky](person:e267fbb1-0d16-45d6-88c5-2bae4738a1dc) mentions a package came for [b]Penelope[/b]… Dum- dum - duh….

articles.title: Session 27
articles.content:
[h2]Marita, O Marita ...[/h2]
First thing in the morning, as we sit at a table eating breakfast, @[Antonio Berensten](person:ec8f50d3-84f6-48a6-95d0-d7d6b5b65cb8) enters. “She’s gone, they took her, you have to find her.” 

He and @[Marita Maggia](person:7c9dab1a-8221-41ca-83f6-0d3b49331e37) were going shopping at the market, near @[The Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b) Market. They were supposed to meet at 8:00 or so. When she didn’t show, he ran by her house, which looked like people were in a frenzy. He then came here.

He takes us to the @[Maggia family mansion](landmark:3b8a3106-f69c-48c1-bd8d-37399c70f36d) in @[The Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b) (I19). Antonio lives at the Tainley house next door where he is a foster exchange noble. We tell Antonio to head to his house, and we will investigate. We walk past the Maggia mansion, the guards are on high alert. 

We make small talk, but they don’t seem to want to admit anything is going on. We try to get them to let us in, but they don’t want to be helpful. [b]Martin[/b] notices a guard coming in from the house. Seems to be an officer. He claims there is no problem. We carry on up the street.

We call Antonio out, and suggest we go find the go-between, Agatha. (Agatha is an acquaintance of a halfling named Notia). 

Antonio describes a unicorn necklace and medallion that she usually wears. With [b]Eugene[/b]’s help he makes a good description of it.

[b]Martin[/b] casts [i]Locate Object[/i] and gets a ping of something. It is in the @[Geronde Family Residence](landmark:aaa37936-24d9-4028-897c-f461d6e7d60c). It seems to be inside the residence. We send Antonio to look for clues and we go to talk to Lady Geronde about the soul trapping mirror as an excuse to get in. [b]Turin[/b] notes that the grounds are pretty open, with a few trees and gardens and flower beds. South of the house is a walled garden. As we approach the house, [b]Martin[/b]’ spell fades, but he notes the object is likely on the second or third floor.

We knock on the door, and the maidservant asks what we can do to help. We ask to speak to Lady Geronde and she puts us in a side room. Lady Roweena comes down, and we explain the life trapping mirror situation. She offered to let her take care of the mirror. We describe the creature and she is interested. She asks us to drop it off in the morning.

On the way out, Eugent tries to message Marita. “Marita, we are Antonio’s friends, are you okay?” but gets no response. He tries again, same thing.

We go to @[Pirandello's Pastries](landmark:00b96683-e4b2-4357-984d-d6959b1cd496) to [b]Martin's[/b] chagrin.  As it is lunchtime, the store is a little busy, but we wait. [b]Eugene[/b] is entranced by the smells and buys many pastries. 

Eventually [b]Martin[/b] gets @[Jillie's](person:6f0f4057-ddb7-4306-a3df-72a4e1964b51)  attention.  It does not go well.  Eventually [b]Martin[/b] leaves the bracelet on the table and goes to leave and breaks through to Jillie and they make up, sort of. [b]Martin[/b] also tells JIllie about Marita. [b]Eugene[/b] makes a picture of Marita to show to her. She thinks she may have seen her but not recently.

We go to the @[Rose Garden Inn](landmark:d657f21f-881e-4050-bf0b-e5bc103ac61a) to see if we can find Agatha. [b]Arada[/b] becomes Notia. [b]Arada[/b] befriends an older halfling named Emilia who is charming. We all have lunch, but there is no sign of Agatha. 

While we are waiting. [b]Penelope[/b] tells us about the swords that were given to her from her father’s study. They are very nice swords. [b]Penelope[/b] wants to keep them, but she's just a little freaked out. She would like to know who the friend is, but one step at a time.

Around 1:30 we head out and go back to Antonios and go into Tenley Manor.  It is dark. [b]Martin[/b] suggests he needs to tell his father. Antonio sends a servant to try to find him. 

In the meantime, he decides to tell his foster mother @[Stella Tainley](person:18e57ef5-b368-4bea-8392-5dd001a5df14) what is happening. She is a tall-ish rugged no-nonsense woman.  Definitely an outdoors person.  We sum up the situation to his foster mom.  

We also explain about the necklace that is in Lady Roweena’s house. We also suggest that we could send a message to Marita through the wall. She suggests she could talk to Marita’s mother (Grilla), as they are neighbors and all. We told her we will come back tomorrow.

articles.title: Session 28
articles.content:
[h2]... and Olivia and Seraphina ...[/h2]
We ask Antonio’s mom about a fancy gem store to ask about Earthheart Emeralds. She recommends asking at @[U.N. Owen](person:49ee217e-a236-4088-862b-479fbfb138a7)’s store (P16). We head there.

@[U.N. Owen](person:49ee217e-a236-4088-862b-479fbfb138a7) is a middle aged greying human man who is known for his work. We ask if he knows about them, but he says he’d be surprised if there was one in town. It has been years (since he was an apprentice) since he saw one.  We ask about them, and he says artificers can use them as an energy source. He says Olivia might know more. 

[b]Martin[/b] also asks about the unicorn pendant.  He says he thinks that sounds like the thing the @[Sisters of the Unicorn](organization:2408af28-23ec-4ef0-acb6-f29005cbbc58) wear.  It is a religious sect, but he doesn’t know more. [b]Eugene[/b] shows them a Minor Illusion, and he thinks that is it.

We also ask if he hears about an @[Earthheart Emerald](material:63f8cdd2-8d51-4846-996e-19525db18f7d) to let us know at the Griffon.

[b]Martin[/b] tries to recall anything about the Sisters, but fails miserably.

We go to see @[Olivia](person:17bc78d2-213b-481c-8a3b-f2dc64dafe23).  As we open the door, the first thing we notice is that the place has been trashed. There is also a mark of a black hand on the floor.

We call for Olivia and the pixie guy.  No answer. We search the building. The back room looks like someone might have been at a desk and got jumped.

We investigate further and [b]Turin[/b] finds @[Seraphina's](person:16390999-39e0-4310-913a-308aec509a5d) toolkit. [b]Martin[/b] finds an overturned washtub. We flip it over with effort. [b]Turin[/b] notices small gnome sized footprints and some of her clothes. We discuss what to do.  [b]Arada[/b] opens a window and looks around to see if anyone left the back way but the alley way is cramped and unused.

[b]Eugene[/b] looks for the notebooks we left Olivia. Shock of shock, they are not there. [b]Martin[/b] casts detect magic to see if we can find the book, but it is not within 1000 feet of us. [b]Eugene[/b] casts Augury to see if it would be weal or woe to search the tower for Olivia and Seraphina. 

[b]Turin[/b] knocks on doors to see if anyone saw anything. Mostly he gets no answer, but one person brusquely says they saw nothing. 

We go to the tower, to make sure things are okay there at least. It is still being guarded, but the guards are not wearing the crests of the noble way barracks, but instead are wearing @[Shield Barracks](landmark:bbfae2a7-d7e1-41bb-b4ae-e3d962d87abf) crests. They say Swiftsword has given them orders to let nobody in or out.

We go back to the Griffon. On the way back, [b]Martin[/b] familiarizes himself with [b]Eugene’s[/b] spellbook, a ring from [b]Penelope[/b], [b]Turin's[/b] bow, and [b]Arada’s[/b] ring so he can us locate object on them if one of us go missing. 

We go to give @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) a debrief. We tell her about the @[Marita](person:7c9dab1a-8221-41ca-83f6-0d3b49331e37) situation and the fact that it is 60/40 if it is a kidnapping or not.  We also mention Marita is a @[Sister of the Unicorn](organization:2408af28-23ec-4ef0-acb6-f29005cbbc58).

Then we tell her about Olivia and Seraphina and their kidnapping. 

And also how we were going to meet with Lady Roweena to give her the soul trapping mirror. She wants the mirror gone.

And we tell her about the need to find the @[Earthheart Emerald](material:63f8cdd2-8d51-4846-996e-19525db18f7d) and the fact that we need one.  She asks if we want to hunt for it, but we suggest we need to solve the kidnapping first.

We discuss @[The Black Hand](organization:d1a96263-4191-482e-ace6-360d25106f60) and how we need to track them down.  Other than [b]Martin[/b]’s “interview the whole city” idea, we have nothing. @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) says she will talk to other captains and see what she can see. 

We go to bed, tho [b]Martin[/b] waits for @[Jillie](person:6f0f4057-ddb7-4306-a3df-72a4e1964b51), who never shows up.

articles.title: Session 29
articles.content:
[h2]... oh, and Jillie, because of course[/h2]
The next morning we get up to take the mirror to Lady @[Rowena Geronde's](person:c44f3336-7f83-4e89-a5c8-78896cc3f650) store, @[Enchantments Ltd.](landmark:cacaff21-6e3e-4a3c-8028-755cb474b3d1) (H09). We decide to take a centaur trolley for safety's sake.

We go in, and Lady Rowena is there, and appears to have been waiting for us. She takes the mirror and leans it against the counter and unwraps part of it to look at it. She asks us if any of us recognize the young girl. In the mirror is a 10 or-so-year-old blonde girl. She matches the description of the daughter from the toymaker’s journal. 

Lady Rowena says this isn’t a Soul Trapping mirror, but a Mirror of Release.  She thinks the daughter was possessed and this might have cleansed her.  [b]Eugene[/b] reads a glyph to free the girl, and she steps out and smiles and evaporates into dust.

Lady Roweena thinks the girl is now released and free. She offers to sell the mirror on consignment. She offers 40% and [b]Eugene[/b] haggles, and they settle in the middle at 40%. 

We figure we need to look for the missing women.  We know they are unlikely to be sold openly, but are probably being held somewhere. We discuss what to do and come up empty. 

The best guess is to go into @[The Labyrinth](settlement:e05f9a94-a44d-4991-b419-28cae89db0ee) and have [b]Martin[/b] try to [i]Locate Object[/i] on the tower defenses book. We make our way to Brandameyer and Greysoul’s Way. 

Without any other ideas, we decide to check in with Lady @[Stella Tainley](person:18e57ef5-b368-4bea-8392-5dd001a5df14). On the way we stop by @[Pirandello's Pastries](landmark:00b96683-e4b2-4357-984d-d6959b1cd496) to make sure @[Jillie](person:6f0f4057-ddb7-4306-a3df-72a4e1964b51)  is there. She isn’t. She didn’t come into work today. [b]Martin[/b] asks what time she got off work yesterday and is told it was around lunch time.

So we go over to @[Tainley Manor](landmark:740e6ef1-9cfe-4a84-91d7-9c9d47736146)  to check in.  We find ourselves in the receiving room of the mansion again. Lady Tainley talked to @[Graella Maggia](person:8c74d2a0-a09b-4139-a4a1-e51fdb18413f) who was surprisingly untalkative. Something is up but she (Stella) has no idea. She asks if we have found anything. [b]Eugene[/b] tells her there are lots of people missing now.  @[Marita](person:7c9dab1a-8221-41ca-83f6-0d3b49331e37), @[Olivia](person:17bc78d2-213b-481c-8a3b-f2dc64dafe23), @[Seraphina](person:16390999-39e0-4310-913a-308aec509a5d), and Jillie. Also Antonio wasn’t able to find Agatha. We ask Antonio to try to find her today and if he can, to bring her to the Griffon this evening.

[b]Martin[/b] loses it on the Maggia guard. And tells them to let us in to talk to the family. They won’t let us in so [b]Martin[/b] tells [b]Arada[/b] to open the gate.  Somewhere an omnipotent being sighs…

[b]Martin[/b] yells at them more and [b]Arada[/b] finally opens the gate. They try to hold it shut and [b]Eugene[/b] sleeps both of them. [b]Penelope[/b] opens the gate to let [b]Arada[/b] in who rolls them gently out of the way. We move to the house. Team chaos rules.

We head to the house. There is a set of stairs leading to a veranda. [b]Martin[/b] walks to the double doors, and goes to open them.  There is a young man standing there. [b]Martin[/b] tells him there are five missing women and we have a confidential informant saying she is the sixth.

He says he hasn’t seen his sister in two days. [b]Arada[/b] takes over the interrogation. We demand to see her room. He goes to get his father. In for a penny, in for an arrest warrant.  He disappears upstairs and the servants scatter. 

[b]Arada[/b] heads up the stairs to follow, with[b]Eugene[/b], [b]Martin[/b], [b]Penelope[/b], and [b]Turin[/b] following. [b]Arada[/b] barges into a room, and the kid says “dad, they know what’s going on.” [b]Arada[/b] asks what they know about the missing girls. He is stunned by the talk of “girls.”

[b]Arada[/b] gets up on the desk with a double summersault. He admits his daughter is missing and the last he saw her was two days ago. It was two days ago, and she said she was going shopping. The kid is very anti-Antonio. It is clear he knows about the relationship.

[b]Arada[/b] asks about any personal assistant. He says she has a maid named “Maggie”. 

The lady Maggia comes in. [b]Martin[/b] asks if there was any indication of who might have done it. They have no idea.  The last they saw she was leaving two days ago. As far as they know, she left successfully and headed towards @[Boutique Lane](landmark:6f3e4291-b4ff-4380-afd6-00be23bb479d). She might have gone to the perfumery or the dressmaker. Or @[Pierre Farce's House of Beauty](landmark:9e38e036-acf1-418e-877b-19e8f6346f74). He thinks his chief of security should have looked into it by now. He will have Georgio come and talk to us. We tell him we are headquartered in @[The Giggling Gryphon](landmark:29260de7-ef14-4aa9-a123-5d0858f00927) in the North Commons. 

We try to see if there is any connection and ask if Marita has complained about lost time, or is an artificer. 

We are introduced to Maggie, Marita’s personal maid. [b]Eugene[/b] asks Maggie if she knows anything about Marita’s plans. Maggie says she was going to get her hair done and then meet a friend.  Probably Pierre’s. 

We ask to look around the grounds after we are done here. 

[b]Arada[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] go to talk to Maggie in private in the hopes she will be more open.  [b]Arada[/b] plays good cop while [b]Penelope[/b] plays buff cop. Maggie alludes to Marita and Antonio and is relieved to find out that Antonio has nothing to do with this.  [b]Arada[/b] asks if there is anything she can remember that can help, what she was wearing, where she was going, etc.  She was wearing her favorite blue dress, she was wearing her pendant (the unicorn one). The last Maggie saw, Marita was headed downstairs to Pierre’s. [b]Arada[/b] asks if there has been anything unusual, or anyone acting strange. [b]Arada[/b] asks if she saw any sign of a sooty handprint anywhere in her room and is told no.   

The parents say most of the staff have been there for a while, at least a year. 

We talk to a few other staff and nobody seems to have seen anything out of the ordinary. On the way out [b]Eugene[/b] pulls Romano aside and asks if he knows of anyone who would want to keep them apart. He wouldn’t put anything past the @[Young Stallions](organization:7340b23d-2c0c-45fd-8222-13b00aa2760c). 

We leave and find nothing on the grounds.

articles.title: Session 3
articles.content:
We were patrolling @[The Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b) when a woman frantically begged us to chase the winged thing that took her baby "Billy". We got some horses and gave chase.

Part way there, we passed a narrow canyon opening and saw a bag that we all figured was an ambush. Sure enough, we were "ambushed" by 6 angry goblins. We quickly took care of them, though [b][b]Eugene[/b][/b] was knocked out by a goblin arrow. [b]Martin[/b] healed him though.

We followed the baby and came to a canyon trail. We followed switchbacks up to a hill that needed to be climbed. We heard some strange noises as we made our way up the hill which sounded like birds and some kind of canine. [b][b]Turin[/b][/b] looked in the nest and saw three griffon hatchlings and a small dog. [b]Martin[/b] had guessed that Baby was a dog. [b][b]Eugene[/b][/b] slept the baby griffons (and the dog) and we discussed what to do. We grabbed the dog and rode back to town. The woman gave a small polished agate in thanks. She said it will bring us good luck.

EDIT: We later learned that the woman was @[Claudia de la Riis](person:646e36a4-cc41-4a91-8076-5af2dcb007fb). We also found out that the agate reward was a Stone of Protection, adding +1 to AC and to all saving throws.

articles.title: Session 30
articles.content:
[h2]Oh wait, we found Jillie[/h2]
As we go outside the gates, we find a servant type who seems to have been waiting for us. Master Tainley would like to meet us. We follow him to the @[Tainley Family Residence](landmark:740e6ef1-9cfe-4a84-91d7-9c9d47736146) mansion.

We are escorted to the usual waiting area and are joined by Lord and Lady Tainley.

“I don’t know how to approach this subject, it is rather embarrassing”

Our daughter disappeared a while ago.  Back towards the beginning of the year, five months or so ago. Nobody saw her going away. She took some clothes and left. She’s let us know she wasn’t happy in her life and wanted to break away.”

“Stella felt we should let her be. And now with these other disappearances we don’t know.”

[b]Eugene[/b] points out that there are some similarities but also some differences.

They want to at least know she is safe and happy.

As best as they can tell the letters are coming from Haven, but that could be through a courier.

Her best friend was @[Cortina de la Riis](person:bc9967fb-4969-46fd-94cf-0294e48ffb27) (one of Harz and Claudia’s three children). 

If we find her, please pass along that we miss her, and hope she is safe and is welcome back home whenever she wants. Stella says to tell her that her brother calls her “Byebye”

We leave with another item for the stack.

We talk about how to start looking.  If [b]Penelope[/b] was abducted, she would wait for an opening.  If [b]Arada[/b] was trying to smuggle someone out she wouldn’t use the gate, she would go towards the river, probably Lombard to the docks. We also think Jillie would have have gone down Lombard to Nobles Way to the centaur taxis. 

[b]Arada[/b] thinks there is an understanding that the Hand is a bunch of quasi-independent groups working together.We don’t think they are in @[The Labyrinth](settlement:e05f9a94-a44d-4991-b419-28cae89db0ee) or North Corridor.  [b]Martin[/b] thinks it could be the docks.  [b]Turin[/b] points out they could be hidden on a ship.

At the base of Lombard, [b]Martin[/b] tries to Locate Object in the bracelet [b]Martin[/b] gave to Jullie. They cover the area from @[The Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b) to North Corridor and a second chance from @[The Commons](settlement:8ff1c19a-c29d-4050-9c7c-7200f23453be) to @[The South Docks](settlement:e2e38a16-2c10-443d-b40a-6789aa7c041a), but nothing pings.

We decide to go back to the Gryphon to plan for tomorrow and to wait for the head of Maggia security. @[Porky](person:e267fbb1-0d16-45d6-88c5-2bae4738a1dc) tells [b]Arada[/b] there is a package for her up in her room.  While she checks that, @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) says we need to talk, something bad has happened.

So it turns out we have caused some trouble. Swiftsword got wind of what is going on.  He says someone is accusing us of kidnapping the girl. He’s planning on taking it to the council. We tell her as an aside we have talked to the @[Maggia Family](organization:7157ba84-27ce-44ee-998e-007b1b0b58fa) and their head of security is supposed to give us a debrief.

We also told @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) that we think @[Jillie](person:6f0f4057-ddb7-4306-a3df-72a4e1964b51) has also been abducted, though she is leery of that.

@[Georgio Iffleren](person:915f58ec-b95d-4fdc-8d11-f8c6ca8ff445) enters. We give him an update.

He tells us Marita was seen at  @[Pierre Farce's House of Beauty](landmark:9e38e036-acf1-418e-877b-19e8f6346f74)   for her hair appointment, and disappeared after that. She may have gone from there anywhere and gotten jumped. We discuss the case a bit. 

We discuss what to do for a while.  Scanning is an option, but it is limited. We discuss beating the bushes to check our networks to see if anyone has heard anything. 

We also show images of the Pirate lady and the tattooed man to Georgio to see if he recognizes them. We also ask if he’s talked to any of her friends. There was someone in the @[Horse Market](settlement:3987cb08-4abd-4e55-a582-128df2ff71db). @[Agatha Ravenwood](person:953ec819-bcd5-4a36-8a7e-ba36e3a8afb2). It was Agatha all along. 

So the plan is to check @[The Dell](settlement:e8aa9d8f-68be-4d89-9e11-6ba288e71515) for Jillie, scan part of the city tomorrow, check in with our networks, have Georgio find Agatha, and find the women.

Before bed, [b]Martin[/b], [b]Eugene[/b], [b]Arada[/b], and [b]Penelope[/b] head to @[The Dell](settlement:e8aa9d8f-68be-4d89-9e11-6ba288e71515) to see if Jillie made it. We make it to Jillie’s house on Hillandale. It is a traditional hillside with round doors. Jillie answers the door and wonders why we are there. She says she didn’t go to work because her mother is sick and she’s been looking after her all day.

[b]Eugene[/b] is a little leery and casts detect thoughts on Jillie. [b]Martin[/b] determines she has pneumonia or the medieval equivalent. [b]Martin[/b] stays behind to heal her in the morning while the party goes back to the Griffon. 

When they get back to the Griffon, [b]Arada[/b] asks [b]Eugene[/b] to see if the vest she got as a package is magic.  It is, but he also knows it is beyond his abilities to identify.  [b]Arada[/b] decides to try to attune to it anyhow with the caveat that if it eats her soul [b]Penelope[/b] will punch her out.


[h2]Finally a break. Let's break stuff[/h2]
In the morning [b]Martin[/b] heals Jillie’s mom.  On his way back, he gets a ping on the map for the Tower Defense book. He runs back to the Griffon. They tell @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) that A) Jillie isn’t missing and B) [b]Martin[/b] has an idea where the book is, and hopefully the women are with it. We explain that [b]Eugene[/b] can try to message the women to see if they are there. 

[container:tablebox-80][img:6605049|nolink][/container]

@[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) asks us to let her know what we find before doing anything rash.  We say of course we will. Wink wink.

We head to the area [b]Martin[/b] got the ping. [b]Arada[/b] spots a guy (Blackdrop)  in the bushes and stealthily goes over to investigate. He is a dwarf that know [b]Arada[/b] as Notia. He says there is something he thinks Notia might be interested in.  Meanwhile [b]Eugene[/b] starts the process of trying to message the three women.  

Blackdrop the dwarf says there is way more money in the fabric and clothing store (@[Fine Silks and Fabrics](landmark:123c418e-9d2e-4cf9-affe-234e2cb1317d), L11) cashbox than there ought to be. He says they have been casing it for a while, and occasionally there seems to be someone who goes in who doesn’t come out. 

[b]Arada[/b] takes the party down the street to tell them what she has learned. The fabric store has way too much money to be legit. 

[b]Eugene[/b] has no initial luck with his messaging, but now he knows more he will try deeper.

articles.title: Session 31
articles.content:
[h2]We do something rash[/h2]
We take stock of what we know.

[ul][li]The fabric store is sketch and has more money than it should
[li]The tower defense book is in one of the shop
[li]We are looking for the girls
[li]@[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) asked us to check in before we do something rash[/li][/ul]

We get [b]Eugene[/b] to try [i]Messaging[/i] a bit more to see if we can get a bead on the girls.   At some point he gets a response. In the basement of the clothing shop

[container:indent-20]
“Who is this?” “Eugene”[br]
“We’re trapped in cages”[br]
“At least three prisoners, and I can hear two other voices”
[/container]

[b]Eugene[/b] promises to try to affect a rescue as soon as we can. He also try-ingle-eights using his [i]Message[/i] to determine they are under the street.

We discuss how to help them. [b]Eugene[/b] tells us they are in the basement of @[Fine Silks and Fabrics](landmark:123c418e-9d2e-4cf9-affe-234e2cb1317d). [b]Eugene[/b], [b]Arada[/b], and [b]Turin[/b] are going to stay behind and keep an eye on things, and [b]Martin[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] are going to let @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) know what is up.

[b]Martin[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] tell @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) we found the girls and where they are. She tells us the store is owned by @[Adrielle Ilorim](person:5d239bee-e2a5-4952-b7ad-412596ca7c50) who is from a relatively well off but quiet family.  They are minor elven nobility.

@[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) tells us to go back and she will join us in an hour. She orders us to not do anything until she is there.  She and [b]Martin[/b] discuss the possible circumstances that we might decide to do something.

Meanwhile [b]Arada[/b] looks to do a walk around and to see if she can climb up to the roof without being noticed. [b]Turin[/b] looks to see if there is a secret access from the green space, but finds nothing. [b]Eugene[/b] narrows the search a bit tighter.

On the roofs, [b]Arada[/b] explores. The roof is lightly pitched. She finds a good hiding spot to keep an eye on what is going on. She notices that part of the roof is about two feet taller in the front than in back. On the backside, there are a couple of windows. A quick peek suggests it is a workroom with four people in it.  Two in the far corner are sewing, two others at different stations.

[b]Eugene[/b] uses his [i]Augury[/i] spell to get an indication if it will be a good idea to enter @[Fine Silks and Fabrics](landmark:123c418e-9d2e-4cf9-affe-234e2cb1317d) to use their basement to get to our friends, (Is it weal or woe to enter @[Fine Silks and Fabrics](landmark:123c418e-9d2e-4cf9-affe-234e2cb1317d) to use its basement to access our caged friends located within 90 ' south of me) but the spell is inconclusive. [b]Turin[/b] goes to the river’s edge to look around. He sees no obvious opening, sewer or otherwise.

[b]Martin[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] return.  We let [b]Turin[/b] and [b]Eugene[/b] know @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) has told us to wait for an hour. [b]Arada[/b] moves to the top of the building and tosses an iron spike to get our attention. [b]Penelope[/b] notices [b]Arada[/b] on the top of the roof. 

[b]Eugene[/b] also confirms that @[Olivia](person:17bc78d2-213b-481c-8a3b-f2dc64dafe23) and @[Marita](person:7c9dab1a-8221-41ca-83f6-0d3b49331e37) are also down there. [b]Martin[/b] casts light on a couple rocks so there can be light in the basement. [b]Eugene[/b] lets [b]Arada[/b] know we are waiting for @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de). [b]Arada[/b] has one more peek at the seamstresses to confirm they don’t look like they are there against their will. At some point checking in with [b]Eugene[/b], she lets us all know.

It has been an hour and still no @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de). [b]Eugene[/b] tries another [i]Augury[/i] knowing there is a good chance it might fail. It says we need to go through the store.

[b]Martin[/b] suggests we go in, get the girls to make a distraction and yell and we “hear” them. [b]Arada[/b] suggests she lures them out.  

The plan is for [b]Arada[/b] to go in the window, [b]Turin[/b] and [b]Martin[/b] to go in the back door, and [b]Eugene[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] to go in the front. [b]Eugene[/b] gives us a one minute warning. 

[b]Eugene[/b] gives the go sign. [b]Arada[/b] bursts in and runs downstairs. [b]Turin[/b] and [b]Martin[/b] search the back room.  [b]Penelope[/b] tries to buy a dress. Something she can wear to a wedding.  With a sword belt.

[b]Arada[/b] and [b]Turin[/b] search and find nothing. [b]Penelope[/b] finds a lovely dark green fabric that would looks great with her hair. The clerks are confused. [b]Martin[/b] explains to a clerk that we’re doing something rash. 

[b]Eugene[/b] talks to another clerk asking where the basement is. A clerk really wants to get [b]Penelope[/b] into the change room and out of her armor. Really wants this. She’s totally into [b]Penelope[/b]. [b]Penelope[/b] disagrees.

[b]Turin[/b] realizes the change room is a good place to hide a secret door. [b]Martin[/b] runs into the change room and something sounds odd with the floor. 

[b]Eugene[/b] runs upstairs and casts [i]Detect Thoughts[/i]. He gleans a sense of fear that we’ve found out. He shouts that he’s found someone who knows something. [b]Arada[/b] runs upstairs. The upstairs folk are confused (except for mistress)

[b]Turin[/b] senses there is a hollow in the floor under the carpet. He pulls up the carpet and finds a trap door. He opens it up and goes. [b]Penelope[/b] yells that we found a door so hopefully [b]Arada[/b] and [b]Eugene[/b] hear and also goes to the changeroom. The horny clerk gives a look of “that’s what I was trying to let you know”

[b]Eugene[/b] tells [b]Arada[/b] that Mistress is guilty and involved. As he interrogates her, she seems confused about the abducted girls.  He pushes deeper into her mind and finds she is involved in kidnapping people, but is confused about the prisoners downstairs. [b]Arada[/b] tells Mistress she is under arrest.  Mistress fails to be intimidated. [b]Eugene[/b] confirms that Mistress is @[Adrielle Ilorim](person:5d239bee-e2a5-4952-b7ad-412596ca7c50).

In the basement we come to a room with a desk and some cages. [b]Arada[/b] sees a bad guy and fires an arrow. [b]Eugene[/b] informs the bad guys that they are under arrest and resistance will be met with lethal force.

[b]Martin[/b] gives [b]Turin[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] an emboldening bond and shouts that we’re here to do something rash.

[b]Turin[/b] splats the bandit who tickled [b]Eugene[/b].  The bandit captain attacks [b]Eugene[/b]. [b]Penelope[/b] does a lot of damage to the bandit captain who is questioning the choices that brought him here. [b]Arada[/b] misses while [b]Eugene[/b] magic missiles him. [b]Martin[/b] does one point of sacred flame asking him to do us a solid and surrender. [b]Turin[/b]’s magic arrow trick does more damage.

More bandits arrive. Yay target rich environment.

[b]Penelope[/b] turns into a whirling dervish and hurts the bandit captain badly and [b]Arada[/b] finishes him off. We start working on the other bandits. 

They try to hit [b]Arada[/b] with their crossbows and miss. [b]Martin[/b] tries to convince them to give up.  It appears to work on one of them. [b]Turin[/b] kills one of the bandits who seems to still be active. [b]Penelope[/b] splats the other. [b]Arada[/b] kills the last guy, somewhat to [b]Martin[/b]’s chagrin.

articles.title: Session 32
articles.content:
[h2]Alas Poor Torik[/h2]
As we look around, we see five people in cages. We recognize Seraphina, Olivia, Marita, Agatha, and someone we don’t recognize. They don’t know where the keys are kept, but fortunately we have an [b]Arada[/b].

As [b]Arada[/b] opens cages, [b]Eugene[/b] searches the bodies. 

The other person is a young boy. He is very eager and thankful. He grovels at us being big damn heroes. His name is Torik.

He says he’s been here a few days. He says there has been a parade of people coming in and out. He comes from a fishing village down the coast (Valtalar which is also where [b]Turin[/b] is from) and stowed away on a sailing boat. [b]Turin[/b] takes an interest. His parents were Frederick and Martha who ran a small general store. [b]Turin[/b] didn’t know them well, but knew they had a boy.

Torik met some “friends” when he got to Haven only they jumped him, and put a bag on his head. He offers to be our house boy. 

We also notice a second set of stairs going up.  Which is a thing for after we get the captives to safety. We also find a black hand. 

Olivia pipes up that she thinks the tower wants us to help us fix it.

We decide to take the captives to the Gryphon and dispatch runners to their houses to get them. We also ask if any of them know why they were taken. Olivia and Seraphina think it has to do with the tower. Agatha and Marita have no idea but were taken separately.

[b]Penelope[/b]’s “girlfriend” upstairs says the rest of the clerks ran away. We ask her to voluntarily come with us. [b]Eugene[/b] and [b]Arada[/b] check upstairs but everyone is gone. We also learn Ardielle is a sponsor of the unofficial watch in the Outlands (which is where most of the other races live.)

[b]Martin[/b] goes to the nearby guard station to get a couple Watch to stand guard while the rest go back to the Gryphon.

At the Gryphon, Porky wants to know what is going on.  When we explain, he gets the staff to help. He sends Alene to get Thom.

[b]Arada[/b] reads the book she got from the basement.

[b]Eugene[/b] conducts a communal interview. 

Torik was the first there and was brought in with several others who were taken away after a day. Then Olivia and Seraphina together, then Marita, then Agatha.

Janie says she has been working at the shop for several months, most days. She had found the trap door a bit ago and thought it was odd. She hadn’t seen anyone coming or going.

We killed six bandits. They think there were four to ten more who all came and went. The leader we killed was the boss we think.  There was a tiefling and a dwarf and at least one woman.

We also check where they got captured from. The one name we get from them is they heard someone mention someone named “Angel”

Eventually they all sign the document (Torik marks an X)

Georgio arrives to get Marita (we also spy Antonio who is not being subtle). They take Marita and Agatha home.

Meanwhile [b]Martin[/b] gets a few guards. He also shows the sergeant the basement and they discuss Adrielle and how if someone was running a slave ring then having an independent guard would be a good thing.

Janie gives us an address near the docks. Olivia and Seraphina decide to stay at the Gryphon for a day or two.

We take a short rest and [b]Arada[/b] finishes the book. She finds the boy is the last of “Lot 32” and they had to get rid of some people to make room for some “special orders” (Marita and Agatha). Olivia and Seraphina were throw ins.

Also the thieves' guild is cranky with the Black Hand.

[b]Arada[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] go to talk with some of her old friends, and [b]Turin[/b], [b]Eugene[/b], and [b]Martin[/b] go to the store.  They take Torik because why not.

At the store, the sergeant is there and there are guards at the front and back. We go to the basement, with the sergeant and Torik in tow.

The stairs lead down and there is a twisty passageway.  There is a pile of brush and shrubs.  Outside we hear the sound of the river. [b]Turin[/b] opens the entrance. We come out on the riverbank just upriver of the shop. It looks like long ago this was a legitimate place to tie up a boat. We can tell it has been used for that more often. It definitely looks like this is intended as a hidden entrance. During the day you probably couldn’t find it unless you were looking for it. 

We decide to hide and see if anyone comes. Getting a couple live captives would be nice.

In another part of town, [b]Arada[/b] takes [b]Penelope[/b] to the guild house. They go to the Blue Moon Tavern. The barkeep used to be active until he aged out of the business and now runs the bar.

[b]Arada[/b] chats up the barkeep and tells him she wants to talk to someone about new events with the Hand. He goes to find a muckity muck

He says Arquephus (Keeper of Records and the right hand of the guildmaster) wants to talk. [b]Penelope[/b] comes with her. She tells him that the Hand has been running a trafficking ring and shows him the log book. She tells him the special package was two wealthy people in the Heights. The Keeper is concerned.

She speculates the Hand has been using the owner of Fine Silks and Fabrics as a stooge. There is a reference to the Speedy Blade special commission. (Speedy Blade is totally not Swiftsword).  [b]Arada[/b] lets it slip that Swiftsword might be the head of the Black Hand…. 

Back on the river bank, Torik is bored.  [b]Turin[/b] tells him he can go play in the Plaza. [b]Martin[/b] gets him a rock for tracking purposes.

At the Thieve’s Guild, they ask [b]Arada[/b] what the Irregular’s plans are. 
[ol][li]Make sure the slave run stays shut down.  
[li]Ask for the Guild’s help watching to see any unusual boat traffic.[/ol]

[b]Arada[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] go back to the store and decide to follow to see where we went. [b]Turin[/b] hears a noise from the tunnel and notches an arrow. [b]Turin[/b] sees a light in the tunnel. [b]Arada[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] sees something outside the tunnel. [b]Eugene[/b] and [b]Turin[/b] who have darkvision recognize them. Crisis averted.

We compare notes. Especially the Swiftsword conjecture. We would have expected a shift change by now. We decide to go back to the Gryphon and talk to some of the nobles in the morning. We have decently good connections with three noble families and at least one merchant family.

As we go to grab Torik, he has gone wandering, probably into the woods. [b]Turin[/b] and [b]Eugene[/b] try to locate him through tracking and magic. He went into Erenhir which is known to be off limits-ish. It is also 2-2:30 in the morning.  [b]Martin[/b] and [b]Turin[/b] decide to crash at the local barracks to pick up the search in the morning.

articles.title: Session 33
articles.content:
[h2]The Irregulars Get an Important Message[/h2]
[b]Martin[/b] asks the Sergeant if he has a couple guards who are particularly bad gossips, and if so could they be on the crew that helps clean up the basement. And it would be a shame <wink> if they spread rumors about how Adrelle isn’t so awesome and is running a slavery ring from her basement. Time to take her reputation down a peg.

The next morning…

[b]Turin[/b] and [b]Martin[/b] wake up and go to Summ Plaza. [b]Martin[/b] casts Locate Object to find the stone he had given Torik. It is a dense wood, but moderately passable. Given that the Elves who live in the woods don’t like non-elves, [b]Turin[/b] goes in alone and [b]Martin[/b] waits.

At the Griffon, [b]Eugene[/b] goes downstairs to see if there are more potions.  There is another one (TP1) , yay! It is red in color with gel chunks floating in it. It smells like wine.

As [b]Turin[/b] gets close, a couple Elves ask what he’s doing. [b]Turin[/b] explains he’s looking for someone and they say that sounds like the kid we’re missing. They melt back into the woods and [b]Turin[/b] gets closer. Eventually he calls out to Turok who responds and looks bedraggled. They return to [b]Martin[/b] and we all go to the Griffon.

They grab a couple to go breakfast sandwiches to go. We discuss plans. [b]Martin[/b] suggests talking to the nobles might be a good start. We also have the log-book as evidence. [b]Penelope[/b] points out that we are good with them as long as their interests are aligned with ours. 

We walk towards the Heights with [b]Arada[/b] following at a distance to see if we are being watched. 

As we get to North Caravan Street, we hear a couple horses steam as if startled and charge towards us. [b]Turin[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] go to help a mother and their child. [b]Turin[/b] picks the child up. [b]Penelope[/b] grabs the mom who is frozen in panic. [b]Penelope[/b] drags her to the side. [b]Eugene[/b] sleeps one of the horses and physics starts to happen. The left horse falls causing the second horse to stumble. The wagon lurches and rolls, spilling several barrels, one of which lands on [b]Martin[/b]. 

[b]Martin[/b] gets up and tries to calm the horse down. [b]Penelope[/b] and [b]Turin[/b] reunite mom and child, and [b]Eugene[/b] sleeps the other horse. [b]Martin[/b] heals the most dangerous of its injuries. We start cleaning stuff up, curious why nobody has come to claim the wagon. On the side are the faded words “C ... ransport Company”. Sadly there is no bill of lading. [b]Turin[/b] realizes one of the barrels “broke open” and seems to be flour.

[b]Turin[/b] tries to track it back to Stone Bridge. He sees where they got startled, but there is nothing there now. We take it to Mint Station. [b]Eugene[/b] puts in a secondary claim that if nobody shows up to take it, we want it.

We head back to the Heights again. [b]Martin[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] have a casual conversation about the benefits of being a caravan guard.

We get to the Maggia household and are let in.  We find Gilberto is there but the others are not. We fill him in on our findings and suspicions. And the fact that Lyza is missing, and we believe Swiftsword may be involved.

He says that it would be useful to know how the guilds feel about us and what we are doing. He offers to talk to the other guild masters. He also says we should talk to Tenley. He will arrange a meeting with Hars de la Riis. He also asks how much we make, which gets a wide range of opinions, but factually 12 gold a week which he feels is too little. [b]Eugene[/b] points out that too little makes it too easy to bribe a Watch. Which leads to a discussion between [b]Penelope[/b] and [b]Arada[/b] about if they are supposed to take bribes.

Next we go to Tenley’s. But we hear a voice behind us, and we see running up to us breathless Aline the maid from the Griffon. Eventually we calm her down. Normally she is soft spoken, but he is rambling a fair bit.  Porky says we have to come back to the Griffon right away. 

[b]Eugene[/b] casts detect thoughts and sees that her mind is a whirlwind of thoughts. He goes a bit deeper and she is relieved she did a good job finding us. 

We figure it is either a trap or that Porky has learned where Lyza is and head back immediately after stopping for pastries. [b]Arada[/b] goes ahead and has a few minutes lead on us, figuring her natural dexyness will save her if it is a trap. Nobody is in the front room, where Porky usually is.  Mrs Clever is in the kitchen. 

She finds Porky and he says he thinks he knows where Lyza is, in the Undercroft of the Grey Halls, which is the main prison. This is where they put people they want to disappear.

[b]Eugene[/b] messages [b]Arada[/b] who says it is safe to come in.

Porky fills us in. He has a contact inside the jail. This contact has also heard rumors that bad things are happening down there, strange noises, the usual.

Things we know about the Undercroft: It is the part of the prison where they put someone who they want to disappear.

Things we have heard: There is a hidden entrance from the outside. 

[b]Arada[/b] thinks maybe the Guild would know where the entrance is. We hang out at a nearby tavern, the Green Griffon (no relation) while [b]Arada[/b] goes to the Blue Moon Tavern (guild HQ).

[b]Arada[/b] tells the person on duty that she needs to get into the Undercroft. The contact thinks she will be able to do that but needs an hour. [b]Arada[/b] also arranged for a few people who resemble us to be there.

The Grey Halls is an old fortress that has been converted into a prison. There is one entrance designed to be hard to get through. There are four floors, and a basement, and somewhere below that is the Undercroft.

While we wait, we level up.

articles.title: Session 34
articles.content:
[h2]Define "stupid" exactly . . . [/h2]
[b]Martin[/b] sends a message to @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) “@[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de), we understand you are in the Undercroft. Can you confirm? We are working on a plan. Hang tight. P.S. It’s [b]Martin[/b]” He gets a reply: “I think I am. Don’t do anything stupid. I can hear chanting and strange noises.”  [b]Martin[/b] doesn’t pass along the “don’t do anything stupid part.”

A shady looking guy enters the bar and greets [b]Arada[/b]. They chat briefly and he passes something to her.  When he leaves, [b]Arada[/b] explains that there is a shift change at midnight, and there is a brief opportunity to go over the wall, into the prison proper, and on the ground level there is a main hallway and a trap door going down. 

<map segment - north part of keep>

They only know of the one entrance to the Undercroft, but there is a rumor of another one. The wall is 25 feet tall or so. [b]Arada[/b] regrets not having a grappling hook. Pondering, she reaches into a pocket in the back of her vest and pulls out a foldable grappling hook. [b]Martin[/b] also expresses a desire to avoid killing any guards unnecessarily and for us to try to be the good guys if we can.

We take our tabards off and head to @[The Grey Halls](landmark:29cb4deb-4fef-475f-8c31-0d8e6f188cda). [b]Turin[/b] casts Pass Without a Trace to make us stealthier. We look and don’t see anyone at the top of the walls. [b]Arada[/b] climbs up, stumbling briefly. [b]Turin[/b] follows, and they pull up [b]Eugene[/b]. They pull up [b]Martin[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] shows off to climb up herself, immediately falling to the ground. A second time, we all make it to the top. We remember to gather the rope and hook.

[b]Arada[/b] listens at the door and cautiously opens it. We enter. [b]Turin[/b] heads down first due to darkvision, with [b]Arada[/b] following. 

<screen shot>

On the first floor, there are three doors, one with a view down the hallway. They see a guard down the end of the hallway. Time to be sneaky.  [b]Turin[/b] gets the rest of us. [b]Martin[/b] recommends quicksaving.

[b]Arada[/b] opens the door we were told about and slips in. It is a storage room with nothing stored. [b]Turin[/b] notices the ground sound hollow and finds a hidden trap door.  [b]Arada[/b] doesn’t find any traps and opens it up. 

<screen shot>

There is an iron ladder leading down.  There is a landing with a set of double doors. [b]Arada[/b] hears heavy breathing like someone has been running, with a touch of panic. We slip in, and there are people there who are not guards. It looks like an intake area with a couple thugs and a cage opening like an airlock.

A thug aims at [b]Turin[/b] and misses. [b]Turin[/b] shoots at the caged guard. And then the other guard. Killing both. There is a logbook that ends yesterday.  [b]Martin[/b] frisks one guard and discovers a Black Hand mark. 

[b]Arada[/b] hears nothing at the next door, which is also unlocked. There is a small hall with two doors. [b]Arada[/b] listens at the north door and hears nothing. The west door and really hears nothing. The door north is locked for sure. 

[b]Turin[/b] hears a voice to the west who says “I don’t know who you are, but I highly recommend you leave. This is not a safe place”. So [b]Arada[/b] unlocks the west door and we go in. It is a guardroom with beds, tables, etc. The next door is unlocked. [b]Turin[/b] and [b]Arada[/b] go in. 

[b]Martin[/b] closes his eyes and listens. There is a sound he doesn’t expect. [b]Eugene[/b] opens up his magical eyes and sees a red haired woman invisible in the southwest corner. It is the woman from the ship and the contest.

<image of redhead>

She says she was sent down here and is leaving. Something has gone wrong and there are creatures. [b]Martin[/b] casts zone of truth to make her talk. She says her boss is the Nameless One. She says she has never seen him. She gets messages. She knows Swiftsword is a customer of @[The Black Hand](organization:d1a96263-4191-482e-ace6-360d25106f60). She is trying to avoid confirming that she is a member of @[The Black Hand](organization:d1a96263-4191-482e-ace6-360d25106f60).

She got the instructions to come here this afternoon at one of the drops. She doesn’t completely know what was supposed to happen. A sorcerer was supposed to try something and it went wrong.  The others with her were grunts. 

[b]Turin[/b] says we are going to take her equipment and tie her up. [b]Arada[/b] takes a step to the side and holds her action. She bolts past [b]Turin[/b]. [b]Arada[/b] produces a couple spikes and a hammer from her vest and spikes the door shut. 

We go back to the other door and [b]Arada[/b] unlocks it. There is another set of double doors. Arade hears the sound of the flutter of wings (large bat or small dragon). It sounds like something being dragged across the floor. [b]Eugene[/b] uses magic to look through the door. He sees a medium sized room, with a few dead bodies, and two small flying lava things [b]Eugene[/b] recognizes as magma mephits. There is also a snail-like thing in the middle of the room with five heads and a multi-hued shell [b]Eugene[/b] recognizes and a Flail snail. 

<snail image>

[b]Martin[/b] says teamwork makes the dream work and we bust in. [b]Turin[/b] launches a pair of arrows killing the close one.  One of the mephits breathes flame on us. [b]Penelope[/b] kills it in response. 

The snail makes five swings against [b]Penelope[/b], hitting twice. [b]Turin[/b] fires at the snail, doing massive damage. [b]Arada[/b] stabs it. Seriously stabs it. [b]Penelope[/b] kills the snail as two more mephits enter. [b]Martin[/b] runs up to heal [b]Penelope[/b] and takes a face full of fire for his trouble. 

[b]Turin[/b] kills the second mephit. [b]Arada[/b] charges the other one and stabs it. [b]Eugene[/b] hits it with a fire bolt causing it to explode in a 36 point critical.
