[b]Penelope[/b] and [b]Turin[/b] check the corridor for cells. They are all empty, though [b]Penelope[/b] finds a plant. 

[b]Arada[/b] picks the lock on the other door. Inside is another snail thing. Two snail things. [b]Martin[/b] casts spirit guardians which does max damage.  Who knew.

<screen shot>

[b]Turin[/b] crits it with his first shot, utterly smacking it.  

The remaining snail attacks [b]Penelope[/b] doing major damage. [b]Martin[/b] steps up to heal [b]Penelope[/b] and do more spirit guardian damage. [b]Arada[/b] damages the snail for hurting her [b]Penelope[/b]. [b]Turin[/b] finishes off the snail, and then the last mephit. 

[b]Eugene[/b] sees another snail in the distance and fires at it. Two more mephits also joins the fray. We pick off the snail before it can close. One mephit gets killed, and more show up.  [b]Martin[/b] blocks the door with his spirit guardians. 

When the last one dies, it explodes, hitting [b]Arada[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] for moderate damage.

We still haven’t found @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de), but we’ve made progress at least. And [b]Martin[/b] has nine more minutes of spirit guardians…

articles.title: Session 35
articles.content:
[b]Turin[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] search the next corridor but find nothing. We keep exploring, and [b]Eugene[/b] sees another flail snail. [b]Turin[/b] runs up and shoots it, and [b]Arada[/b] stabs it and runs away. The snail runs up to flail at [b]Arada[/b] knocking her out. [b]Eugene[/b] drags her back and [b]Martin[/b] heals her,  killing the snail with his Spirit Guardians.

Looking down the hallway, we see a large vaguely humanoid shambling thing that appears to be made out of dirt and rock. 

<image of earth elemental>

[b]Turin[/b] decides to shoot it, doing some damage.  We think.  [b]Eugene[/b] webs the room it is in. [b]Arada[/b] shoots it with her bow, and [b]Martin[/b] briefly puts it into his glowy death circle. The creature gets stuck in the web, giving us a chance to shoot it like a fish in a barrel. 

Turns out really nasty monsters who spend the entire fight restrained are not as tough as they ought to be.

We investigate the room, that looks like it used to be a torture chamber which has been converted into a summoning circle.  There is a dead body to the side wearing robes. It appears to be the summoner. The body has a spellbook and components for summoning elementals.

The body looks familiar to us. We recognize him from the competition. The formerly dead rust monster summoner.  The mystery deepens.

We go to the remaining door which is locked. There is no sound coming from the other side. [b]Arada[/b] opens it, and there seems to be another small corridor and locked door which [b]Arada[/b] also unlocks. Inside is an unconscious Lyza.  [b]Martin[/b] uses the last of his healing to try to get people topped up, including Lyza who is unconscious on the floor. It seems to be a room to hold a slightly more valuable prisoner.

We can probably carry Lyza out, especially since she doesn’t have her armor or weapons. [b]Penelope[/b] carries Lyza so [b]Turin[/b] can keep his hands free if we need.  On the way out, we also check the summoning circle to make sure it is disturbed. [b]Eugene[/b] does a sweep and doesn’t find anything magical. 

We go back to where we left the ship captain who is not here. We discover a trap door we did not see before. We open it and see a ladder going down. At the bottom is a passageway leading off in some direction. Our best guess is this passage goes south. 

We follow the tunnel, which appears to be rough hewn and put here for a purpose. It goes quite a ways. [b]Turin[/b] finds a new set of fresh tracks (probably the ship captain). After maybe 400 feet or so we find another ladder going up. [b]Arada[/b] climbs up and finds herself in a small cave where the far end is covered in brush and shrubbery. We exit through the opening. The elves think they can see Gallows Hill to the south of us. We are outside the city wall.

<screenshot / map>

Near us is a cart and a horse. The horse looks like a good strong draft horse, and the cart is in good shape. [b]Turin[/b] uses a spell to talk to the horse. The horse says a big guy brought him out and left him there. We assume it is help from [b]Arada[/b]'s friends.

We put our tabards back on and take the horse and go towards Horse Gate.  We get back unchallenged and the guards let us in with a weird look. It is around 2am, so the cart bridges are closed but we could get back to the Griffon on foot. 

We leave the cart at the Southbridge barracks, and go across the pedestrian bridge.  We don’t think we are being tailed as we return to the Griffon. 

We get Thom to look at Lyza. She goes into ger ‘er done mode and sends [b]Turin[/b] and [b]Penelope[/b] to get the spare bed and take it to Thom’s lab.  The rest of us carry Lyza down. And for the first time we get to see Thom’s lab. We get Lyza settled in as Thom tries to figure out what has happened. 

We get her settled, and go up to rest.

articles.title: Session 36
articles.content:
Starday morning . . .

Since he doesn’t need to sleep as much, [b]Eugene[/b] studies the spellbook.  The book contains 4th level spells, putting them at 7th or 8th level.  Otherwise we get rest. Thom has tried things with Lyza, but nothing much has changed.

We get up, and haven’t seen hide nor hair of Porky, but we do hear sound from the kitchen. [b]Eugene[/b] comes up from the basement. Before [b]Martin[/b] can go downstairs, there is a pounding at the door with a deep voice “Open up in the name of the City Guard.” We look out the window and see at least 20 people.  One of the people is Swiftsword. They are all wearing Heights tabards. 

When we got up, we saw the door was barred.  We have a quick discussion about what to do. 

He demands we turn over Lyza who is being accused of treason. [b]Turin[/b] has a quick look out the back. There are more guys out back. He finds a hiding spot to keep watch.  We quickly run over the people we could try to contact.  Lucille, Lady Roweena, Hars de la Riis, the Mayor, Gilberto Maggio, Tenley Tainley. 

[b]Martin[/b] send a Sending to Hars de la Riis, telling him that Lyza is being accused of treason and we are holed up in the Griffon.  He says give him 5 minutes. Swiftsword says we have until a count of 10.  [b]Eugene[/b] casts his hut spell to try to block the door. [b]Turin[/b] casts a spike growth in the middle of the back to try to block it off.

They start to hit the door with a battering ram. They break the bar, but the hut prevents the door from opening.  They realize there is something blocking the way. We hear a smash as one of them breaks a window. This is getting messy.

[b]Arada[/b] starts running around causing mayhem without trying to get into combat. [b]Martin[/b] yells to Mrs Clever who says she doesn’t know where Porky is. More guards start to smash windows. [b]Penelope[/b] tries to block a window.  [b]Arada[/b] carries on with the mayhem. 

[b]Martin[/b] yells that we won’t let them take Lyza to the Black Hand and that we know he’s connected to them, and we’ve given the proof to the council of guild leaders.  Swiftsword bellows. We suspect we aren’t going to last five minutes.

[b]Arada[/b] taunts Swiftsword who is not happy. [b]Penelope[/b] keeps pushing them out windows. [b]Martin[/b] casts Heroism on [b]Penelope[/b] and takes a critical for his trouble.  [b]Penelope[/b] is pissed. [b]Arada[/b] downs one of the guards outside and jumps through a window to get back inside. [b]Penelope[/b] downs the guard who hit [b]Martin[/b]. 

Swiftsword decides to join the combat and misses.  The guards out back start to advance and enter [b]Turin[/b]’s spike growth.  The spike growth does major damage, and [b]Turin[/b] shoots one. The door to the cellar where Lyza is is also out back, so holding it firm to keep them away from her is vital.

Still, as long as the bad guyscontinue to follow the wisdom of J’hakee Chann and come at us one at a time, we might have a chance.

[b]Arada[/b] hears a voice in her head saying “get everybody down”.  She drops to the ground dragging [b]Penelope[/b] with her.  

From the grassy area, she sees a streak of lightning jump out and hit one of the guys. And another one.  And another one. And another one. And another one. Somewhere D’hee Jhay Khallid is pleased. Lady Roweena has entered the fray, and she is pissed.

Out back, the guys are unsure what to do with the spikes. So they keep advancing, because it has to work eventually.

[b]Penelope[/b] knocks Swiftsword out with a surge of action.

[b]Martin[/b] tries to convince them to surrender and drop their swords and testify against Swiftsword.  He gets three to do so.  [b]Eugene[/b] does something similar and gets one more to surrender.

Outside Roweena uses her lightning again, and the front rank die.[b]Penelope[/b] can see the end of the street and sees Porky with more guards. Out back, [b]Turin[/b] sees more guards being led by Lucille. She calls them to drop their weapons. They do, and [b]Turin[/b] drops his spikes.

A third lightning bolt finishes them off, accidentally killing Swiftsword. At least it wasn’t us.

In the aftermath, we have rounded up 16 members of the Heights watch, who are carted off to jail by the watchmen who are there. They refuse to talk to us, and are quiet being carted away.

In the aftermath, Swiftsword’s body is dragged into the bar, and we talk with the cavalry.

They were led by Porky, Roweena, Lucille, and Hars. Hars apologizes it took so long.  

The story comes out that after we went to rescue Lyza, Porky went to Hars, who went to Roweena, and also talked to some of the guard and Lucille at the suggestion of Porky.

[b]Martin[/b] goes to tell Thom who lets him in. Lyza is sitting up, and is groggy. She is awake but not totally here. [b]Martin[/b] also casts Protection from Poison on her to help cure her. He and Thom start taking her upstairs.

We ask if people had suspicions about Swiftsword before this. They tell us he was always resistant, but they thought it was out of jealousy.  Nobody is sure how many of the Heights watch is Black Hand. They promise to take appropriate steps to find out how far this is spread.

Lucille speaks up and says she thinks the Market Barracks are clean and points out most of the Market Barracks guards came to help.

[b]Penelope[/b] wonders aloud what was happening in the Undercroft which is a surprise to the cavalry. She explains the summoner and mephits. [b]Eugene[/b] starts explaining the Undercroft.

Thom, Lyza, and [b]Martin[/b] come upstairs.  

Lyza looks at Swiftsword’s body and wonders what happened. Roweena explains we tried to keep him alive, but stuff happened. We don’t think he has family, certainly not in Haven.

[b]Martin[/b] explains the human trafficking ring under Fine Silks run by Adrielle Ilorim. And that Swiftsword was using them.  Hars asks Lucille to lead the investigation into Swiftsword and what he may or may not have been doing.  He also says Lady Roweena may know some people who could help. Hars reminds us about the tower.

[b]Turin[/b] also makes sure she knows about the trap door.

We also tell her about the double dead wizard fellow, which raises some eyebrows.

Roweena leaves with the body and Hars leaves with Lucille.

We decide to get some breakfast. [b]Martin[/b] breaks out the Mending and Carpenter’s Tools as Thom heals the rest of us.

articles.title: Session 37
articles.content:
We discuss what to do.  [b][b]Arada[/b][/b] thinks the Guild would know where Swiftsword hung out. We should return the cart, repair the griffon, investigate Swiftsword’s house, investigate the Tower, or go shopping.

We decide to go shopping.

We go to @[Southbridge Barracks](landmark:45f9cb91-00a7-4466-9872-b2d5339cccb9) to get the horse and cart and take it to the Guild. They said they took it to Paddy’s Wagons, so we go there. It is a wainwright, and we see the wagon we found last night. They give us the wagon, but they don’t have the horse. So we go back to the barracks to ask about the horse. They don’t know, but will ask when he gets back.

So back across the bridge to @[The Mint](landmark:2697e796-f355-4ec8-966e-d86062d89b5b) Barracks, to ask them about the other wagon and horses. They said that nobody claimed it, so they took it to @[Ironhoof Stables](landmark:d08be9da-ef3e-47d2-9e59-a609397adff0).  They give us a chit, and we go there.

So we go to @[Ironhoof Stables](landmark:d08be9da-ef3e-47d2-9e59-a609397adff0) which is just outside Copper Gate.  It is a fairly large fenced in area with a pair of stables, small smithy, and a paddock out back.  

We meet the guy in charge. He and [b][b]Turin[/b][/b] talk horses a bit. We also give the wagon paperwork to him and he tells us the draft horses are out back. He says he can hook [b][b]Turin[/b][/b] up for 80 gp or so, plus the cost of tack.  

A stablehand brings out a bay for [b][b]Turin[/b][/b] to try out. [b][b]Martin[/b][/b] thinks it is a decent horse, and [b][b]Turin[/b][/b] casts speak with animals and it seems to be a content horse. The second horse is a little smaller, but also a good, slightly above average horse. [b][b]Turin[/b][/b] goes with the bay named “Ace” and tack and a military saddle.

[b][b]Arada[/b][/b] talks him down to 77gp for the package. We also buy a week's worth of feed for all three horses. [b][b]Martin[/b][/b] suggests calling the two draft horses “horse one” and “sassafras”.

We head back to the Griffon and unload the cart and stable the three horses and [b][b]Turin[/b][/b] gives Torik the quick “how to take care of horses” instructions.

We take one of the draft horses to get the other wagon and take it back. [b][b]Arada[/b][/b] and [b][b]Penelope[/b][/b] take the cart back. [b][b]Arada[/b][/b] talks to them and they say they will get her a packet of information about Swiftsword.  And that they didn’t leave us a cart and horse.

With all the travelling, it is suppertime, so we go back to the inn for dinner and park our other cart and horse one. 

@[Porky](person:e267fbb1-0d16-45d6-88c5-2bae4738a1dc) says we had a messenger from one of the noble houses who left a heavy box that clinked. We take it upstairs to look through it. [b][b]Penelope[/b][/b] carries it and [b][b]Arada[/b][/b] and [b][b]Eugene[/b][/b] look for traps.  We see on it the crest of the @[Maggia Family](organization:7157ba84-27ce-44ee-998e-007b1b0b58fa).

[b][b]Arada[/b][/b] opens it, and inside there is a lot of gold and platinum and a note in an envelope on top. The note says

[container:indent-40]“Consider this part reward and part downpayment - GM" [/container]

There is about 1800 gp in gold and platinum. [b][b]Martin[/b][/b] says that a 300gp diamond for Revivify would be useful and [b][b]Eugene[/b][/b] would like a component for Clairvoyance. (which we acquire offline). [b][b]Arada[/b][/b] also shows her bracelet that she stole to [b][b]Eugene[/b][/b] and best they can tell it is “very rare”

[b][b]Eugene[/b][/b] also identifies the magic armor that came off the dead wizard. It is a replica of true plate made of paper. It is “scroll plate” which is sort of mage armor in armor form, but has some cool side effects.

articles.title: Session 38
articles.content:
Over breakfast  the next morning the door to the Gryphon comes flying open.  A watchman we don’t recognize comes bursting in and says there has been a raid at the tower and we need to come at once.  We tell @[Porky](person:e267fbb1-0d16-45d6-88c5-2bae4738a1dc) to let @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) know we’ve gone to the tower. The watchman says he was at @[Mint Station](landmark:3e7f73d4-077b-43d4-bb83-4a5beefc5d8d) and his Sergeant told him to get us.

When we find ourselves outside the tower, where we expect guards, we find none and the door was wide open. It looks like they have been broken open. We enter carefully but confidently. We enter the lobby and see a pile of dead bodies and destroyed clockwork spiders.  The people are all wearing leather armor without tabards. It looks like a band of ruffians. We do a quick search.  Leather armor, cheap weapons, money, black hand patch.  The usual.  We loot a small amount of coin and [b][b]Penelope[/b][/b] gets 36 pounds of short swords.

Fortunately @[Tick and Tock](person:98040fff-4014-41dc-b234-0f005a4e944f) are still here.  Also some of the things we repaired seem to be now broken.

We first go up to the Timepiece of Eternity with the guardian. More bodies (more used swords!), but the Guardian is still around. [b][b]Eugene[/b][/b] flashes the clockworker’s medallion and the guardian seems mollified.  Atop the pedestal in the middle, there is a series of five small circular indentations on the surface. They look like they may hold some type of gemstone. Earthheart emeralds maybe?

<<insert screenshot>>

In talking to the guardian, it seems to follow [b][b]Eugene[/b][/b]’s basic commands.

We go to @[The Foundry](landmark:bf4238e7-34eb-47da-a388-a1f4b9ff93ea) where Beep Beep is, and find …  yep, more bodies.  They look like they have been stacked by Beep Beep. The ingots that Beep Beep made are still there. So we move to the next room…

We go to the room of mirrors where we met the ghost of Gerhart. We recall the ghost wanted us to repair the mirrors.  [b][b]Martin[/b][/b] is pretty sure that Mending won’t be effective. The party discusses asking the mayor if we can borrow a few of the mirrors for the council chambers

In the Assembly Room we find more bodies, which is unusual since nothing was lethal in here before.  

We go to the library and find a few more bodies. It is looking to us like they sent in a bunch of mooks who managed to all die.

We start looking for any useful books, but find nothing. With nothing else to do, we decide to talk to the mayor and get Olivia.

We make our way back to the watchmakers shop, and Olivia and Seraphina are there. We tell them we are going back to the tower, via the mayor’s office. We explain to the mayor that we need some mirrors to repair the tower.  She also conspicuously avoids any discussion about Swiftsword.

We take a pane of glass across the road to see how it fits.  Sadly we think we will need a glass cutter to make it work. Fortunately Olivia has a glass cutter. [b][b]Arada[/b][/b] tries cutting the glass, which turns out to be a piece of cake. We fix it into one of the mirrors on the floor, and the room seems to brighten ever so slightly.

We repeat the process for all the major mirrors.  Once they are all back in place, the ethereal figure of Constantine forms in the shimmering reflection of the newly restored mirrors. His translucent visage contorts with a mix of relief and despair. "Adventurers," he begins, his voice echoing through the chamber with a hollow resonance, "my Clockwork Tower, a marvel of time and engineering, now stands on the precipice of collapse. I need your aid to right my wrongs. "In my zeal and ambition, I left much undone. As you ascend, you will find my research, incomplete designs, and abandoned constructs. The time control device, my magnum opus, remains unfinished, a testament to my hubris. Seek the blueprint, lost in the maze of gears and pendulums. Only by completing my work can you restore harmony to the tower's temporal core. "Time, while a fascinating subject of study, is an unforgiving master. The tower groans and grinds, its once seamless operations marred by decay and neglect. Every jammed door, every grinding gear, is a cry for help. "The very heart of the tower flutters erratically, like a bird trapped in a cage. Power surges and wanes, causing rooms to vanish, constructs to go awry, and time itself to warp." His voice grows desperate, "You must find an alternative source of power or stabilize the existing source." He looks at you, hope gleaming in his spectral eyes, "Help me set things right. Restore the Clockwork Tower to its former glory. Only then can I find peace, and the tower continue its journey."

We discuss where the maze might be, and Olivia and Seraphina offer to fix some of the basic stuff. We show them the library and the book inside the glowing force field. [b][b]Eugene[/b][/b] does a perusal of the library to see if there is anything of interest, but find nothing.


<<insert screenshot>>


Since it is afternoon, we decide to go back to the Griffon, making a brief detour to sell the weapons we looted. [b][b]Penelope[/b][/b] sells the swords and daggers, and gets a total of 194 gold. That’s a lot of beer money. Or ink money in [b][b]Eugene[/b][/b]’s case. We also hire someone to repair the door and get the guards from Noble Station to resume guarding.

Also while we are there, we learn more about the aftermath of Swiftsword.  The story they heard was that Swiftsword and some of his guard tried to take us down and there are rumors of some very high ranking people who came to help us.  What we hear is half true, but also some exaggeration.  One of the big questions is “who is in command now?” Right now, each local Captain is watching their own area, but they are waiting to see what next.

It is clear there is some support for us on the ground, but we don’t know what kind of support Swiftsword had. [b][b]Eugene[/b][/b] also talkies to one of the Noble Station wizards about setting up a "Police Wizarding Best Practices” committee. They think that is a great idea and offer to set up a meeting.

Back at the Griffon, @[Porky](person:e267fbb1-0d16-45d6-88c5-2bae4738a1dc) tells us @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) is in front of the fire. Thom tells us to keep things calm and quiet.

We talk about Swiftsword and the updates about the Tower.  Between [b][b]Martin[/b][/b] and [b][b]Eugene[/b][/b] we totally keep her calm.

She tells us she went to Swiftsword and got jumped by some of his thugs and we go through the events.

She also tells us Hars left a message for us with @[Porky](person:e267fbb1-0d16-45d6-88c5-2bae4738a1dc).  He says the message is that the long and short of it is that with the gaps in the structure of the city and that some of the powerful groups are looking for us.  And if there is something we could do out of town, we might want to do that.

[h2]Wedding or Mine Hunting time![/h2]

We throw out the wedding idea, and realize we should leave tomorrow or the day after.  He thinks he can get us some supplies if we can make a list.
[row]
  [col3]
    [ul]
      [li]Tents
      [li]Blankets
      [li]Horse blankets
      [li]Tarps
      [li]Rations 
    [/ul]
  [/col3]
  [col3]
    [ul]
      [li]food for the horses
      [li]Mobile kitchen
      [li]water / water containers
      [li]Wagon repair tools
      [li]Basic tools, shovel, axe, hatchet, 50’ of rope
    [/ul]
  [/col3]
[/row]

[b][b]Penelope[/b][/b] can talk to contacts if there is a caravan going that way

We also discuss planting false rumors where we are going.

[b][b]Turin[/b][/b] can procure some food on the way via hunting to supplement.

As the crow flies it is 150 miles to @[Oakridge](settlement:7ea6d7eb-ee01-4ebb-9e88-226dca2f1246), which will be 6-8 days with the wagon.

articles.title: Session 39
articles.content:
[h2]18th of Wealsun, 1172[/h2]
Before we leave, [b]Eugene[/b] goes to one of the Watch towers to help set up the Wizard of the Law support group and ask them to help spread the word of how to help, things like sleep, web, sharing spells.  He is also spreading rumors where we aren’t going.

[b]Martin[/b] visits the temple of Ea to make a prayer for safe travels and for Ea to bless us on our journey.  He spends the rest of the morning going over the wagon to make sure it is safe for the trip. 

We also made a supply list for the trip:
[row][col3][ul]
  [li]Tents (or are we using the tiny hut?)
  [li]Blankets
  [li]Horse blankets (3)
  [li]Horse hobbles (3)
  [li]Bedrolls (5)
  [li]Tarps
  [li]Frame to cover wagon?
  [li]Rations (2 weeks worth for 5 people?)
  [li]map and compass
[/ul][/col3][col3][ul]
  [li]Food for the horses (2 weeks worth for 3 horses?)
  [li]Mobile kitchen
  [li]Water / water skins
  [li]Wagon repair tools: Hammer, saw, prybar, plane, chisel, nails, 2-3 iron bands
  [li]Basic tools: shovel, axe, hatchet, 50’ of rope
[/ul][/col3][col3][ul]
  [li]Torches
  [li]Pickaxes
  [li]Large chest to store tarps, bedding etc, 
  [li]Large chest to store  tools
  [li]Locks for chest (2)
  [li]Small keg of ale 
  [li]50’ of rope and block and tackle
  [li]Small barrel of apples
[/ul][/col3]
[/row]

On the way out of town, [b]Eugene[/b] swigs one of the mystery potions from Thom. TP-17.  He feels like he really wants to get into a fight. He thinks he would be better at it, though who knows if he would or not.  It also tastes like urine mixed with raspberry.

In the early afternoon, we head out towards Caravan Gate at a normal pace. On the way, [b]Martin[/b] thinks we’re being followed one block west. He has [b]Eugene[/b] send a message to Turin who peels back to follow the person on Ace.  [b]Martin[/b] turns to take a different street and the mystery man backtracks a little. 

[b]Martin[/b] is positive we are being followed, but not sure who. He thinks it could be walking, riding, or hiding above. He tells [b]Arada[/b] to watch the roofs, [b]Penelope[/b] to watch riders and [b]Eugene[/b] to watch pedestrians.

He sees Antonio on a street corner.  [b]Martin[/b] tells [b]Penelope[/b] to see what he wants and [b]Martin[/b] will bring the cart back in 15 minutes. Antonio ducks down an alley and [b]Penelope[/b] follows. [b]Penelope[/b] feels someone brush against her and hears a whisper from the alleyway “you didn’t see me, I’m not here, go away.”  [b]Penelope[/b] turns around. [b]Arada[/b] saw Marita brush against [b]Penelope[/b]. 

[b]Martin[/b] makes a loop and picks up [b]Penelope[/b]. [b]Penelope[/b] checks her pockets but doesn’t find anything.  [b]Martin[/b] still thinks we are being followed but we head out Caravan Gate, rejoining Turin on the way.

At Caravan Gate, we see Jillie and Torik.  Jillie wants to know where we are going. Torik wants to know why we left without him. Jillie wants to know if she can come with us.  [b]Arada[/b] sees Torik try to sneak onto the wagon and [b]Penelope[/b] holds him down.  [b]Martin[/b] talks with Jillie and promises to send messages as much as possible.  He also promises to bring her a present.  Torik says we should hold him down so he has to follow. Eventually Torik gets browbeaten into staying. In all this, we gather a large crowd.  Best sneaking out ever.

As we go through the gate, the gate guards recognize us and tell us to have fun.

We spend some time on the road, using the last few hours of daylight. At least we don’t see any sign of being followed.

We are on the road and realize we are going to have to probably camp out at night. We find a good spot about twenty feet off the road. The weather is overcast and chilly, and we decide to set up watches. [b]Eugene[/b], Turin, then [b]Penelope[/b] and [b]Martin[/b] (aka the can’t see in the dark crew).

Turin also starts training Ace to simple commands.

Through the night, nothing untoward happens.  


[h2]19th of Wealsun, 1172[/h2]
The next morning, we continue along and spend another day on the road. On the second night, the main caravan route continues North, and a smaller road turns towards Oakridge.  There are a few ruins where a settlement might have once been.  We expect the night to be a bit chillier.

Before bed [b]Martin[/b] checks in with Lyza who comments on our stealthy way of getting out of town.

Similar watches, with [b]Arada[/b] joining middle watch. 

During the second watch, they hear howling in the distance to the east.  Best they can tell, it is wolves that are quite a distance away.


[h2]20th of Wealsun, 1172[/h2]
The next day we continue east, passing the occasional farm with the river to our left.  We pass the odd hamlet.  Towards noon, we come to a town. 

It has the standard issue taller fence walls, but the streets are mostly empty and it smells run down.  The few villagers we see glance furtively, and seem eager to move from place to place.

There doesn’t seem to be an inn, though there is a tavern, when we go to for lunch.

The bored tavern keeper leans against a post.  Through small (very small) talk, he tells us the town’s name is Tarnsworth and we should leave.

He says the town is cursed, and the howling is driving folks mad.  People go missing or get killed. Animals have been killed, the usual. He says it has been going on for a few weeks. It started small, but now the howling happens most of the night. It seems to be coming from the southeast.

He says we could talk to mayor Gorgeson, but he is in mourning since his son died three days ago. We have a small conference and decide the appropriate response is “big damn heroes time”.

We eat lunch and decide to go see the mayor. After some wandering through the town, we see a church and a mansion.  Mostly people look at us and close their windows, but Gredel, an old half-elf woman, comes running towards us. She asks us if we are here for the guard work for her cows, milk and cheese.  Cream got eaten. 

We tell her we will come to her place after we talk to the head cleric. We enter the church, and meet a middle age tiefling woman and ask for more information about the howling.

We get similar information from her. She says it used to happen once a blood moon (i.e. every few years), but now it is every night. 

We go to Gredel’s and she greets us at the door.  She takes us to the back yard, with a fenced off area and three stalls. She says a few nights ago the howling got close, and she heard a scream and a commotion.

The door to the stall is ripped off and there are deep claw marks.  Turin takes a look and the tracks are paw prints, but this looks like bipedal.  Turin hypothesizes some kind of were creature. The tracks lead through part of the stone wall that has been knocked down.

We leave Ace and the draft horses at Gredels and head south. We eventually reach the woods, which is wet, dense, and without a path.  

Turin leads us through the woods. Along the way he finds a tarnished cowbell that says “Cream”.  It is getting short on daylight, so we decide to head back.  As we get to the edge of the woods, we hear howling, and growling behind us.  We double time it back to the town.

Gredel thinks we will have time to get the horses to the church stable. We do so, and come back to watch her cows. The wagon stays outside, though we do take the meat we have into the stables.

We set up an ambush area and wait for the woofs.

articles.title: Session 4
articles.content:
We are patrolling near the docks at night. We heard a crash and saw a wooden ship that had crashed into the jetty. The dockmaster, indicated that he heard the crash, but we see no people. The ship is called the Nightmare. [b]Penelope[/b] remembers a rumor "Did you hear about the ship that was lost at sea, The Nightmare? They say that it was carrying a fortune in gold and jewels now everyone is looking for the wreckage."

We told the dockmaster to stay back and if he sees guards, to have them set up a perimeter. We started exploring the ship. The ship is eerily quiet. Fortunately [b]Turin[/b] is familiar with ships, so he is able to lead things.

On the back, looking at the steering wheel, we saw blood where something had been dragged. Moving to the front of the ship, we saw what [b]Penelope[/b] identified as ballista. There is no shrine to @[Nenya](person:928a98b6-5cca-47d7-82cc-78f092e55d65), the goddess of the sea, which is unusual.

The captain's quarters are luxurious, but normal. [b]Arada[/b] tries to open the sea chest. It is filled with women's clothing. Is the captain a woman?

The front of the ship is the officer's mess. There is an overturned inkwell that has dried on a map. The meal is cold, but not rotten. It looks like they were interrupted mid-meal.

We go to explore down below. It reeks of death and decay. Flies, stench. [b]Penelope[/b] hurls at the stench. [b]Martin[/b] ritually casts Detect disease while [b]Eugene[/b] makes some masks. The crew cabin in the front lower deck looks like it is a massacre scene. Hard to tell if people lost them in a fight or before the fight. [b]Penelope[/b] found a severed arm.

We explore the back. More signs of combat around the officer quarters. We find a rat body with a large bite out of it.

We open the hatch to the hold. The stench is worse. [b]Martin[/b] tosses a torch down. [b]Arada[/b] suggests we tie a rope to her so [b]Penelope[/b] can pull her up if needed. [b]Arada[/b] goes down and sees six bodies and lots of blood.

Against better judgement, we do down into the hatch and find ghouls. Combat ensues. One ghoul is quickly dispatched, but another gets the drop on [b]Turin[/b] and paralyzes him.

The captain of the ship closed the hatch on us, and wants us to leave. We search the hold and are sure there is nothing there. We agree to leave.

As we leave, @[Lucille Rilossa](person:ce375714-67ed-4fad-810b-bc3b0eaf0fad) (Our guard friend from the other day) runs up and then agrees to go get help. We see the captain on the deck jump off the far side. With nothing left to do, we return to @[The Giggling Gryphon](landmark:29260de7-ef14-4aa9-a123-5d0858f00927) to fill in Captain @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de).
[hr]

articles.title: Session 40
articles.content:
We think we have a small amount of time to prepare for the … wolves?

[b]Martin[/b] gathers a bunch of small rocks and casts light on each of them and hands two to each player. 

We light up the backyard of the house and decide to set up an ambush near some trees. 

<<screenshot>>

[b]Turin[/b] is in a tree, [b]Eugene[/b] is behind a wall.  [b]Penelope[/b] and [b]Arada[/b] are near the wall, and [b]Martin[/b] is hiding behind a trunk.

[b]Martin[/b] hears rustling from the south and [b]Arada[/b] sneaks closer to a bush. [b]Eugene[/b] messages [b]Turin[/b].  [b]Penelope[/b] readies her crossbow. [b]Turin[/b] gets ready to cast spike growth as we wait for them to close in.  

[b]Turin[/b] drops his spike growth. [b]Arada[/b] shoots her bow.  [b]Penelope[/b] fires her crossbow. [b]Martin[/b] throws light rocks.

[b]Eugene[/b] launches a series of magic missiles at the one he can see, and [b]Turin[/b] peppers another with his bow.  [b]Martin[/b] bonds himself, [b]Arada[/b], and [b]Turin[/b], and one of the wolves falls down. Another stumbles out of the spike growth and misses [b]Penelope[/b] as [b]Eugene[/b] had foreseen.  A quick riposte fells it.

The last wolf stumbles out and attacks [b]Martin[/b]. It misses the bite, but claws [b]Martin[/b] for ten points of damage. [b]Arada[/b] sneak attacks it, but it takes a little less damage than she expected. But [b]Eugene[/b] finishes it off. 

We look at the bodies. They look like bipedal wolves. From the myths and such we’ve heard, it looks like the hybrid form of a werewolf. As we understand it, the hybrid should be the most dangerous. 

[b]Martin[/b] heals himself a little, and detects disease and finds nothing. [b]Martin[/b] does however hear howling from the other side of the village. And we are off. 

[b]Arada[/b] rushes to the front and [b]Eugene[/b] casts fly on [b]Turin[/b].  [b]Penelope[/b] sets off to the south and  [b]Eugene[/b] takes the road through town. [b]Martin[/b] starts running.

On the west side, [b]Arada[/b] sees more werewolves causing mayhem.  [b]Turin[/b] quickly catches up and drops a spike growth on one in a courtyard. It goes to chase after [b]Arada[/b] and starts taking spike damage. [b]Turin[/b] starts firing from the air.  And [b]Eugene[/b], [b]Penelope[/b], and [b]Martin[/b] keep running.

[b]Turin[/b] blasts two of them, and [b]Arada[/b] lures the remainder to [b]Penelope[/b] and [b]Eugene[/b] who hits it with a massive fire bolt. [b]Penelope[/b] finishes it off.  [b]Martin[/b] keeps running.

[b]Turin[/b] uses the remainder of his fly to do a perimeter search of the town.  [b]Martin[/b] is still running.

Eventually we meet up and discuss what we know about werewolves.  Which is very little.  We drag the bodies towards Gredel’s house near the other were-bodies. 

We discuss what we are going to do.  Which is probably to sleep for the night and search for the were-lair tomorrow. [b]Martin[/b] walks just out of town and listens.  He hears the faint sound of a howl and two response howls.

[b]Eugene[/b] uses a sound illusion to make a howl and gets an indeterminate number of responses.

We decide to take our usual watches. [b]Martin[/b] touches base with @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) and @[Jillie](person:6f0f4057-ddb7-4306-a3df-72a4e1964b51) .

Come the morning Gredel sees us.  She wants to know what happened. We explain the wolves in the town are dead, but there are more.

We discuss what to do and decide to go hunting the lair.

articles.title: Session 6
articles.content:
[h2]19th of Planting[/h2]
Evening of the 19th, we come back from patrol and hear (who we learn to be  @[Mikyl Swiftsword](person:403bf324-8208-4b8f-98d1-8e98ded30ba5) ) arguing with @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de).  Mom and Dad are arguing about us.

[h2]20th of Planting[/h2]
We did some shopping. We bought a pearl.

[h2]The Escape Dungeon (21st of Planting)[/h2]
The day of the event we go to the Trademaster's Square, where we wait for a long time. We are in with a group of other adventurers in @[The Hall of All Guilds](landmark:b0995c90-f747-40b6-994b-c052b8b4f278), who all are waiting their turn. It is a mixed bag. [b]Turin[/b] heard a familiar female voice, but couldn't locate the speaker.

We had our choice of six different items, fish, stones, parchment, a d8, a broken key, and some salt. We were told to only take one each but [b]Arada[/b] took two!

There were many puzzle rooms, gather three keys and open a door, solve a puzzle with an owl bear, pick out the right door (we picked a tree. Shouldn't have picked the tree), and solve a logic puzzle.

We ended up third, which was the best result for a guard team in years. The winning team was called the Enigma. A red headed woman, a wizard with a yellow beard, and a heavily tattooed man.

At the celebration, [b]Arada[/b] heard the voice of the captain from the Nightmare - it was the read head from The Enigma! Dun dun dun.

On our way home, walking through the streets, at the corner of Nobles and North Caravan we feel a bit unsteady. [b]Arada[/b] falls over. [b]Penelope[/b] and [b]Martin[/b] notice a weird sensation, like someone is pulling us backwards. We feel like we are in a haze. The haze passes, and we find ourselves in the middle of a celebration. We are back in Trademaster square. We decide to take the same route home. We take the same route and get back to the Griffon.

@[Porky](person:e267fbb1-0d16-45d6-88c5-2bae4738a1dc) is at the bar reading today's riddle:
[center]In halls of stone where echoes sleep,[br]
A guardian of secrets, its vigil keeps.[br]
With no eyes to see, no ears to hear,[br]
Yet senses all who venture near.[/center]
What am I?

We guess potato, small rock, spells, traps but the actual choices were:
[ul][li]A) A ghostly whisper [li]B) A magical rune [li]C) A stone golem [li]D) A living shadow[/ul]

articles.title: Session 7
articles.content:
[h2]Us and Julio Down by the New Guards[/h2]
It is early morning. We are patrolling near the city guard armory, near @[The Academy](landmark:4bdf469a-ba1e-4ef0-80b4-aa67f33878af). We pass by and Julio is no longer outside on guard. We peek inside, there are carts which would be loaded, but no Julio. "On the wall is a black handprint". [b]Arada[/b] remembers that six months ago the HQ of the Thieves Guild was burned. Many (stupid) people thing the thieves guild is no more. The people in the know think a rival guild "The Black Hand" is involved in a turf war.

[b]Arada[/b] sees something moving to the south side of the main store room. It looks like a giant bug rummaging through boxes. The crates are open and there is some rusty metal near one of them. It attacks [b]Penelope[/b] and misses. [b]Martin[/b] uses magic (and needs to remember order of operations). [b]Eugene[/b] put it to sleep and [b]Penelope[/b] and [b]Arada[/b] smushed it.

And we found another one. Yay. [b]Arada[/b] hits it with her crossbow straight through the … cloaca maybe? [b]Eugene[/b] finished it with a magic missile. And there is a third. [b]Penelope[/b] disengages and [b]Arada[/b] shoots it. [b]Eugene[/b] hits it with fire. [b]Turin[/b]g shoots it and [b]Martin[/b] sacred flames it. It tries to rust [b]Penelope[/b]'s armor and hit her. More fire, and [b]Turin[/b] kills it.

There is some munched metal, but we stopped most of it. There is nothing obvious about how they got in.

[b]Martin[/b] goes to get reinforcements. We roust the Sergeant and the Captain.

A civilian says she say some shifty men who attacked the guard and had a horse and cart. Her husband thinks it may have been the work of the Shadow Rats, who are a small group of petty criminals. He's heard a rumor of a new crime boss who is organizing the small gangs into a big gang. He mentions a yellow bearded wizard. Sounds like our Enigma friend.

[b]Martin[/b] suggests getting someone to alert the nearest guards barracks and to ask the trolleys if they have seen anything.

We head towards @[Market Barracks](landmark:8b369554-047b-483f-8486-6460bd10c977). @[Lucille Rilossa](person:ce375714-67ed-4fad-810b-bc3b0eaf0fad) is on duty. We fill her in on what happens. We offer to let her be a local to help up. She comes with us and suggests to start at the Rat Statue (P03). We make our way to a dingy little alley way where the statue is (the Bronze Rat Memorial). We hear a door close behind us and someone went running. We lost them.

We go back to look through the house they came out of. The house is abandoned, but also has been used. The footprints go throughout. [b]Arada[/b] unlocks a door leading to stairs down. We search and upstairs we find the tortured body of Julio. [b]Arada[/b] is mad. [b]Martin[/b] is really pissed off.

We went down in the basement and [b]Penelope[/b] sneakily discovered a mimic. It chomps her. She swings back. [b]Martin[/b], [b]Turin[/b], and [b]Penelope[/b] are down there and join in. Lucille and [b]Eugene[/b] rush downstairs. We make quick work of the mimic. In the remains of the mimic, we find a chewed up body of a man with a yellow beard. In the room, we discover a pair of gauntlets and a stack of papers. And some really weird bones.

The runes on the ground look like a summoning circle. Maybe this is where the rust monsters came from?

[b]Turin[/b] reads the papers (in elvish). It iooks like someone what hired by someone referred to as "The Nameless" to disrupt @[The City Watch](article:b6a3cf89-af08-4102-abe7-bf68c1f9bd94). Whomever it was was trying to summon rust monsters. Also a kobold, mephit, and a quasit.

We secure the crime scene.

On our way back home, we meet a man who is concerned and wants to talk to us. He is acting on behalf of an employer looking to perform a little task. Nothing illegal, but from members of the guard. If we wish to pursue the matter, we can meet on God's Day the 25th at 9pm at @[Duhm's Rooms](landmark:f83b9e6a-745d-459a-b363-a6759cb707bd).

articles.title: Session 8
articles.content:
[h2]The Debrief[/h2]
We debrief  @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) She wants to know if [b]Arada[/b] knows anything about the Hand.

We also told her that [b]Penelope[/b] needs new chainmail. @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) says we should go to @[The Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b) and talk to @[Kelvar](landmark:3187fc00-afbf-4c19-b15a-037f7139f700)  the armorer.

We also told her about the side job offer. With the meeting at @[Duhm's Roohms](landmark:f83b9e6a-745d-459a-b363-a6759cb707bd). She says we should go, not commit to anything and report back.

We also tell her about @[Lucille Rilossa](person:ce375714-67ed-4fad-810b-bc3b0eaf0fad) and her new status as an irregular irregular.

[h2]A Very Spotty Problem[/h2]
We have patrol near the copper gate and @[The Commons](settlement:8ff1c19a-c29d-4050-9c7c-7200f23453be). We have been acting as gate guards, learning the basics of commerce. Near the end of our shift, we see a solitary figure approaching. It appears to be an old woman. We seem to recognize her as a woman who was breeding dangerous animals and got expelled. She seems to have a baby red dragon on her shoulder.

[b]Eugene[/b] goes to talk to the Barracks captain next door. [b]Martin[/b], [b]Penelope[/b], and [b]Arada[/b] go to the road to talk. [b]Turin[/b] hangs back. [b]Martin[/b] says she can't come in without a license for her cat, or other pets into the city. She casts a curse on [b]Martin[/b]. something something spot on head, rainbows, blah blah, you're dead.

Coming back, [b]Eugene[/b] notices the four of us have orange dots on their foreheads. [b]Eugene[/b] goes to talk to her. She curses him too. [b]Eugene[/b] doesn't know what the critter is, but can look it up. Another guard says something about a new curse running around and spots and such. And we should look around the @[Thieves' Market](settlement:8b3bf96f-ea9a-4e39-8c3f-bda804df5cdb).

[b]Martin[/b] casts Detect Poison and Disease and finds nothing. [b]Eugene[/b] casts detect magic, and all the spots are magic which is Abjuration. We decide to go back home to talk to Thom. @[Porky](person:e267fbb1-0d16-45d6-88c5-2bae4738a1dc) says Thom is down in her lab. @[Porky](person:e267fbb1-0d16-45d6-88c5-2bae4738a1dc) thinks there is a drink that might break the curse. We talk to Thom in the basement. Thom says it is orange. She also says she isn't good with curses.

We change out of our guard uniforms and head to @[The Waterlogged Rat](landmark:7d581473-a0bb-406c-b81d-ef9bc7233858) (U08) in @[The Commons](settlement:8ff1c19a-c29d-4050-9c7c-7200f23453be). It gives off the vibe of a dive bar, dimly lit, dingy, smoke filled. [b]Turin[/b] thinks he has been there once or twice. As we stand in the entrance, we get a queasy feeling and we find ourselves walking down the street towards @[The Waterlogged Rat](landmark:7d581473-a0bb-406c-b81d-ef9bc7233858).

We sit at the bar. The barkeep thinks we are in the wrong bar. We ask about the spots, and the barkeep gets freaked out. He calls over Mike the dockworker who says we are screwed. Joey says we need some of the juice to drink. A shot of owlbear urine stirred with an awakened tree branch. [b]Eugene[/b] buys a round of drinks. The stories started a week or so ago, shortly after the ship came in.

We decide to go to @[Lohisian Place](landmark:6e53f231-e24c-492b-8a38-7cbb7b60641d) (R12). It is a nice clean white stone building. As we walk in, there is a statue of the goddess. We go up to the check in desk. There is a female half elf (Raven) and a male human. They agree to look at us. So far they are very calm. They look at us one at a time. A gentleman (Herman) walks in with a gaggle of students. [b]Penelope[/b] goes first and tells the story. The students react. One tells about rumors on the street. The doctor does comment it seems to get yellower but we should have a couple days.

As we start walking over, [b]Eugene[/b] passes out. The rest of us feel a lurch and a sense of nausea. The rest of us wake up looking out the lights of our own private rooms. Middle of the day, we are dressed. [b]Turin[/b] sends a message to @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) telling her what's happening. We meet with the doctors. Shirin (an elf) is on duty. We go to a meeting room and are told the seniors will be with us soon. Herman and others come in. They tell us they are going to kick us loose for the day and ask us to come back tomorrow. Shirin lets us out.

We go back to the Griffon

[h2]A Shady Meeting[/h2]
We are on our own for patrol. We go to @[The Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b) first to see if @[Kelvar - Armorer](landmark:3187fc00-afbf-4c19-b15a-037f7139f700) can fix [b]Penelope[/b]'s armor. [b]Penelope[/b] meets @[Kelvar](person:bde8ced1-ce85-43cc-982d-d8b9d5597925), who is a Dwarf. Name dropping @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) does wonders. [b]Penelope[/b] shows off the rust monster damage. Kevlar thinks they could fix it, but not sure if it would be as good. But instead has a nice new suit of chain mail. It almost fits, but needs some alterations - maybe a couple hours.

[b]Martin[/b] goes in to talk to his girlfriend, @[Jillie Ferndew](person:6f0f4057-ddb7-4306-a3df-72a4e1964b51) who works at @[Pirandello's Pastries](landmark:00b96683-e4b2-4357-984d-d6959b1cd496), but she isn't in at the moment. Instead he talks to @[Pirandello](person:bdf62286-6824-4b51-99b1-6da3c4469796). [b]Martin[/b] leave's a message for Jillie. [b]Martin[/b] also finds out the spots are yellow-green.

[b]Turin[/b] goes to the weapon store. At some point he plans on finding a bowyer. [b]Arada[/b] is thinking through her contacts to see if anyone knows about the spots.

[b]Eugene[/b] goes to the healer who explains they need the right supplements.

At 7pm [b]Penelope[/b] gets the chain armor. It will be close, but we are off to Dhoom's Rhooms.
